issue_title: Behavior changes based on presense of colorama
issue_body:
Without colorama installed, the provided ColoredFormatter will always use colors, even when redirected to a file (ie. $ ./command.py 2> error_out.txt)

When colorama is installed, colorama detects a non-tty, and strips the colors.

Since colorlog provides a TTYColoredFormater that will strip colors for a non-tty, the above behavior seems not only inconsistent, but unintended. (ie. the user chooses stripping for non-tty by choosing TTYColoredFormater instead of ColoredFormatter, not by having or not having colorama installed.)

I therefore suggest that the colorama init should be called as:
`colorama.init(strip=False)`
or minimally some other option should be available to colorlog users to 'force' colors or prevent stripping.

I like my logs to have color even when redirected to files.  Using 'less -R' let's you still benefit from the colors when reviewing the output.

issue_title: Fetching a list of automated emails is missing query params
issue_body:
For some reason, Mailchimp API v3 official documentation doesn't mention the usual query params for this particular API endpoint:  `/automations/{workflow_id}/emails`.

So in the corresponding mailchimp3 library call: `client.automations.emails.all(workflow_id='')`, it accepts neither pagination query params nor field related filters(`fields`, `exclude_fields`). 

As a result, the results returned from mailchimp are using the default 10 item page. If you have more than 10 automation campaign emails, it will only return the first 10.

I spoke with Mailchimp support, the pagination and field filter query params are global for all the GET calls. 

I will try to come up with a PR for this.

issue_title: create_new_activation_key is insecure
issue_body:
It uses the python `random.random` which is not cryptographically secure and also unnecessarily uses the user's ID in the hash which makes it possible (although improbable) for the same key to be generated for a different user if their IDs overlap. For example, a user is created with an ID, then at some point deleted and a new user is created with the same ID.

I suggest using an algorithm that uses a secure random function to create an entirely random string or at least allow the user to specify their own algorithm.

Such an algorithm may look like this:

```
def create_new_activation_key(self, save=True):
        """
        Create a new activation key for the user
        """
        self.activation_key = hashlib.sha1(os.urandom(32)).hexdigest()
        if save:
            self.save()
        return self.activation_key
```

I am happy to make a PR if this is something you will consider merging?

It seems to be an issue with parsing the `job_id` returned by `subprocess.check_output()` inside of `BcbioPBSPROEngineSetLauncher` (lines 737-739). Passing the argument `universal_newlines=True` will make `subprocess.check_output()` return a `str` and should prevent this error.

issue_title: Stop sorting dicts (at least by default)?
issue_body: Traditionally, `pprint` has sorted dict keys, because dict order was arbitrary so we might as well arbitrarily pick a nice order. But starting in 3.6 unofficially, and 3.7 [officially](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2017-December/151283.html), all dicts are effectively `OrderedDict`s (modulo some details about how `==` works and a few extra methods that `OrderedDict` has). So probably the printer should show this order.

issue_body:

Used by `BooleanOperationManager` at:

https://github.com/typemytype/booleanOperations/blob/e65175badaa304578087cf921724fbc53676cc52/Lib/booleanOperations/booleanOperationManager.py#L89-L90

Seems that removing the function and removing the for-loop (that currently does nothing) from `BooleanOperationManager` would be better than to keep this dead code around.

Alternatively, it's a bug and the early `return` statement was inserted during debugging and accidentally committed.

issue_title: Calling disconnect() will not disconnect the client.
issue_body:
Hi,

I started the client using "wait forever" as described on the README. When calling disconnect(), the session gets disconnected, however,  the **engineio** will still be running and tries to reconnect with a new session.

What is not clear is that the the documentation is not enough if disconnect() is actually for the sessions or for the client.

As for my use case, I patched https://github.com/invisibleroads/socketIO-client/blob/1e58adda9397500d89b4521c90aa06e6a511cef6/socketIO_client/__init__.py#L69 and added 

```python
if self._should_stop_waiting():
    break
```

The code should look like this.

``` python
 def _get_engineIO_session(self):
        warning_screen = self._yield_warning_screen()
        for elapsed_time in warning_screen:
            # added condition
            if self._should_stop_waiting():
                break
            transport = XHR_PollingTransport(
                self._http_session, self._is_secure, self._url)
            try:
                engineIO_packet_type, engineIO_packet_data = next(
                    transport.recv_packet())
                break
            except (TimeoutError, ConnectionError) as e:
                if not self._wait_for_connection:
                    raise
                warning = Exception(
                    '[engine.io waiting for connection] %s' % e)
                warning_screen.throw(warning)
        assert engineIO_packet_type == 0  # engineIO_packet_type == open
        return parse_engineIO_session(engineIO_packet_data)
```

I just want to make it clear if the disconnect() function is only for sessions, if yes, please expose a function for "client disconnection". 

Thanks!

issue_title: Sanic support: buggy request parsing
issue_body:
When trying to use websocket support with git version of `Sanic`, I had a problem: server kept responding with `400 Bad request` when the browser tried to upgrade connection to `websocket`.
In the logs I saw the following:
```
WARNING:engineio:Invalid session 060c95b4c0d54ba5b95665acf1ed751d?EIO=3
```
Turned out that `engineio.AsyncServer.handle_request`  got two values for `sid`, first of which is the wrong and second is correct.
Next, `QUERY_STRING` was taken from the output of `self._async['translate_request']`. And Sanic-specific implementation turned out to be buggy.

In `engineio.async_sanic.translate_request` function (line 46) it takes `request.url` and conditionally appends `request.query_string` to it. But according to [Sanic docs](http://sanic.readthedocs.io/en/latest/sanic/request_data.html), `request.url` holds "The full URL of the request, ie: `http://localhost:8000/posts/1/?foo=bar`"
This bug resulted in query-string being duplicated and URL being treated like this:
`'http://localhost:8000/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=LhzFKOB&sid=c0a70e70d8bc465e9611f5bae89ce083?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=LhzFKOB&sid=c0a70e70d8bc465e9611f5bae89ce083` - notice the duplication.

I will now propose the trivial fix as a pull request.

issue_title: Add option whether always to refresh
issue_body:
Currently, Django Elasticsearch DSL forces an index refresh on every update. While this ensures data is available for search immediately, it also generates a high server load in a write intensive environment, and is just wasting good server power if being able to search that fast is not a priority.

While the `DocType.update` method has an argument `refresh`, most refreshes are triggered by signals which does not support passing that option.

An global option and/or an option per doc type (meta class?) would help prevent unnecessary load on the Elasticsearch servers.

issue_title: Allow adding separate where='sql expression' for PostgreSQL and SQLite
issue_body:
The where='sql expression' is inserted directly into the CREATE INDEX statement when a migration runs. It must be valid for the currently active database backend.

While the django-partial-index project supports both PostgreSQL and SQLite, they have slightly different expected syntax for some expressions.

In a particularly common case, they differ in valid boolean literals: SQLite only accepts 0 and 1 as numbers, and PostgreSQL only accepts TRUE, FALSE and a few string values: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.1/static/datatype-boolean.html

This means that where-expressions for the common case of filtering on a boolean flag need two different expressions in a project that uses PostgreSQL in production and SQLite in testing.

Proposed solution:
* where='' is used for both backends,
* where_postgresql='' and where_sqlite='' can be used instead to provide different expressions,
* Only one of them can be provided if the developer is not aiming to support both backends,
* It's up to the developer to ensure that their behaviour is identical,
* For consistency, where='' cannot be used if either or both where_<vendor>='' are provided,
* For consistency, where_<vendor>='' expressions must be different; if they are identical you must use where=''.

issue_title: hashivault_write reports changes in "update" mode when passing strings for int values
issue_body:
Hey,

There are some places in the Vault API where you can write a string value, but reading returns an int value. For example, the max_ttl value inside a role in the SSH backend. Though the documentation example (https://www.vaultproject.io/api/secret/ssh/index.html#read-role) shows it returning a string, it actually returns a plain integer value:

```
curl -H "X-Vault-Token: ${VAULT_TOKEN}" ${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/ssh/roles/testrole
{"request_id":"ed7b1f29-5f64-0016-9c09-3f214876b26c","lease_id":"","renewable":false,"lease_duration":0,"data":{"allow_bare_domains":false,"allow_host_certificates":false,"allow_subdomains":false,"allow_user_certificates":true,"allow_user_key_ids":false,"allowed_critical_options":"","allowed_domains":"","allowed_extensions":"","allowed_users":"*","default_critical_options":{},"default_extensions":{"permit-pty":""},"default_user":"centos","key_id_format":"","key_type":"ca","max_ttl":0,"ttl":1800},"wrap_info":null,"warnings":null,"auth":null}
```

The way the hashivault_write module checks for changes means that if you write a string value from Ansible, it will always be reported as a change, even though nothing has actually changed.

The obvious workaround is to set an integer value in Ansible instead, but this isn't always possible - for example, when the value is templated (as in our case) it will always be a string, and so will always be marked as changed.

Would it be possible to have the check relaxed slightly, so that the string value is not treated as a change?

It's worth mentioning that there are other cases in which you get a similar "always changed" behaviour due to reads not matching the writes, e.g. when using values like "30m" for a TTL value, which the read returns as "1800". I doubt that's so easily fixable though, as it depends on what field is being written/read, and there are inconsistencies between the backends (as some return "1800" and some return "30m0s")

issue_title: Support boolean outputs for topk operator
issue_body:
## Description
Output from topk operator is positional 0s and 1s. It would be more standard with Numpy NDArray and other DL framework, if the return values are Boolean (True / False).

Similarly, since MXNet NDArray wants to be closer to behavior of Numpy NDArray operations, it would be good to support Boolean types as output for conditional operators.

This functionality will also help Apache MXNet backend for Keras2

issue_title: API documentation out-of-sync with version on website
issue_body:
The version currently listed on https://mxnet.incubator.apache.org/ is 0.11.0 (although 0.12.0 was
just released but not yet shown on the versions list), but the API documentation appears to be for 0.12.0 or master. For instance, the fluent NDArray operators that were added in 0.12.0 appear in the documentation. I observed this on 10/30/2017, before 0.12.0 was released. 

I am not sure whether this is because the site doc was updated prematurely or whether it is
actually tracking master.

This caused some confusion when I tried to use documented methods that were not there.

issue_title: Closing a socket will hang the asyncio poller
issue_body:
When closing a socket the asyncio poller will crash, even when unregistering first: The whole coroutine will be broken, and couldn't find a way to actually catch the exception.

Version info:
```
libzmq-4.1.6
pyzmq-16.0.2
Python-3.5.4 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Aug 14 2017, 13:26:58) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)]
```

Program to demonstrate the issue:
```
import asyncio
import zmq.asyncio as zmq_asyncio
import zmq
import logging

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

zmq_asyncio.install()
ctx = zmq_asyncio.Context()
s1 = ctx.socket(zmq.PULL)
s1.bind('inproc://1')
s2 = ctx.socket(zmq.PULL)
s2.bind('inproc://2')
poller = zmq_asyncio.Poller()
poller.register(s1, zmq.POLLIN)
poller.register(s2, zmq.POLLIN)

async def ping():
    s = ctx.socket(zmq.PUSH)
    s.connect('inproc://1')
    s.connect('inproc://2')
    while True:
        s.send_unicode('ping')
        await asyncio.sleep(2)

async def unregister():
    await asyncio.sleep(1)
    poller.unregister(s1)
    await asyncio.sleep(0.5)  # Increase this to "solve" the bug
    s1.close()
    print('unregister')

async def listen():
    while True:
        try:
            poll_events = await poller.poll(2000)
        except (asyncio.CancelledError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            break
        except Exception:
            logging.exception('Error polling for events')
            continue
        for socket, poll_event in poll_events:
            msg = await socket.recv_unicode()
            print(socket, msg)
        if not poll_events:
            print('no msg')

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
asyncio.ensure_future(ping())
asyncio.ensure_future(unregister())
try:
    loop.run_until_complete(listen())
except Exception:
    logging.exception('error running loop')
finally:
    ctx.destroy()
```
It is expected to keep printing ping after removing and closing the socket. Instead the poller will get an exception and the statement `poller.poll` will never end, traceback:

```
ERROR:asyncio:Exception in callback _AsyncPoller.poll.<locals>.on_poll_ready(<Future cancelled>) at /opt/anaconda3/envs/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zmq/eventloop/future.py:88
handle: <Handle _AsyncPoller.poll.<locals>.on_poll_ready(<Future cancelled>) at /opt/anaconda3/envs/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zmq/eventloop/future.py:88>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/anaconda3/envs/venv/lib/python3.5/asyncio/events.py", line 127, in _run
    self._callback(*self._args)
  File "/opt/anaconda3/envs/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zmq/eventloop/future.py", line 91, in on_poll_ready
    if watcher.exception():
  File "/opt/anaconda3/envs/venv/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 306, in exception
    raise CancelledError
concurrent.futures._base.CancelledError
```

issue_title: How many audio frames are created from one second of audio?
issue_body:
I have a question regarding the conversion of audio to array that is related to the"'tensorflow's ctc cant handle audios that have fewer frames than the target annotation has characters" issue: What is the conversion rate between seconds and audio frames, i.e. how many audio frames do i get out of, say, 1 second of audio; in other words, how finely are the audio chopped up when converted into arrays? Im asking because due to some audio-annotation mismatches in my DIY importer, i seem to get short audios with long (incorrect) annotations. tensorflow says something like: "length of audio (100) is shorter than length of annotation (143)."

now i know that all my audios are at least 2 seconds long (this i have verified). If i knew how many frames (-> "length of audio" from tensorflow's point of view) each second of audio produces, i could catch those audios during preprocessing with simple if "n_character > r_conv * n_secs = n_frames" for each audio segment. is there a fixed conversion rate r_conv? i have looked in the code but i havent been able to find that rate if it is there.

many thanks in advance,

seb

issue_title: Travis sends failure emails although github has green checkmark
Throw more specific exceptions / document attributes
issue_body:
Currently if you were to run code like:
```
# Example from developer manual
# but how do we know this was specifically the `Neo.ClientError.Statement.InvalidSyntax` status code?
try:
    session.run("This will cause a syntax error").consume()
except CypherError:
    raise RuntimeError("Something really bad has happened!")
finally:
    session.close()
```

You would get a `CypherError` which is a catch all for a lot of different errors. Currently you would need to inspect the `code` attribute of the exception to match it up with http://neo4j.com/docs/stable/status-codes.html

Would it make more sense for this driver to check for the various codes and throw a specific error? (in this case maybe `CypherSyntaxError`)

Also, it would be helpful for the docs (or comments in the code itself) to include a mention of `code` and all the attributes we can expect to be set on an exception.

issue_title: Deprecate get_default_value method?
issue_body:
From PR #31 - the `get_default_value` method on traits now just returns `self.default_value` in all the traits we define. Dynamically created defaults (e.g. for `Instance` traitlets) are built via a different mechanism, so that we can avoid creating them until they're needed.

If there are no other traitlets defining or using `get_default_value`, we can deprecate it and just use `self.default_value` instead. Widgets [doesn't appear to](https://github.com/ipython/ipywidgets/blob/master/ipywidgets/widgets/trait_types.py). Are there any other users of widgets we know about?

CC @minrk @SylvainCorlay

issue_title: What is the point of target_table in sqla.SQLAlchemyTarget?
issue_body:
Hi,

I was exploring contrib.sqla for using `SQLAlchemyTarget` without `CopyToTable`. 
(Not sure how recommended this is, but that is not the point here)

I was wondering about the point of the `target_table` constructor argument and instance property.
It is used in `touch()` for insert/update in the marker table
but is not read/checked somewhere (e.g. it could be added as where clause in `exists()`)

For my use case, the target_table is not interesting (I use "dummy"), so I'd like to have it dropped,
but I guess for users of `CopyToTable` covering multiple target tables, this might be a source of bugs (wrong results from `exists()`).

issue_title: CMake Error at CMakeLists.txt:965 (INSTALL):
Add optional before/after kwargs for callables to be run before/after a logic function.
issue_body:
There are occasions where you want some custom code to be run before running a logic function or afterwards in order to customize a response.  We should add the ability to specify an optional callable for both cases that can be specified per route.  For example:

```python
import logging

def log_before_logic():
    logging.debug('Before logic gets called')


def log_after_logic(result):
    logging.debug('After logic function result is %s', result)


def logic():
    return "result"


create_routes((
    Route('/foo/', methods=[
        get(logic)],
        before=log_before_logic,
        after=log_after_logic
    )
))
```

                    part_info = {'PartNumber':part_number, 'ETag':part['ETag']}
                    etags[str(part_number)] = part['ETag']
                    parts.append(part_info) 

            s3.complete_multipart_upload(Bucket=bucketName, Key=self.key, UploadId=self._uploadId, MultipartUpload={'Parts':parts})
```

I believe that this issue has popped up several times in other AWS SDK's related to uploading large files to S3. I have tried performing this action through a proxy to see if my network reliability was bad. No luck. I have tried changing the chunk size, no luck. I have tried changing the file size. No luck. 

Any thoughts?

issue_title: DBClusterIdentifier is implicitly lower cased in restore_db_cluster_to_point_in_time and describe_db_clusters
issue_body:
when calling:
```
rds.restore_db_cluster_to_point_in_time(SourceDBClusterIdentifier=resource.conn_id,
                                                       DBClusterIdentifier=resource.identifier,
                                                       RestoreToTime=point_in_time,
                                                       VpcSecurityGroupIds=[security_group],
                                                       DBSubnetGroupName=subnet)
```
`DBClusterIdentifier` is implicitly lower cased. This leads to a very unfortunate bug that is propagated in:
```rds.describe_db_clusters(DBClusterIdentifier=resource.identifier)``` and `DBClusterIdentifier` is implictly lower cased again, returning "valid" results. 

Finally, where teh bug bites:
``` rds.create_db_instance(DBInstanceIdentifier=reader_instance,
                                          DBInstanceClass='db.r3.8xlarge',
                                          Engine='aurora',
                                          DBSubnetGroupName=subnet,
                                          DBClusterIdentifier=resource.identifier)
ClientError: An error occurred (DBClusterNotFoundFault) when calling the CreateDBInstance operation: DB Cluster not found
```

Using the exact same value in the previous 2 function calls fails here because `DBClusterIdentifier` is _not_ implicitly lower cased at this point.

issue_title: Generating recurring events
Purpose of this package: API or I-feel-lucky Plone Solr integration?
issue_body:
While polishing this package for a Plone 5 compatible release I found myself asking the purpose of this package. Should it be

1. an API for plugging in other (UI) products or
2. a full fledged solution for all relevant Plone-Solr integration tasks?

I'm tending to go for 1. because of the following reasons:

With an interface to Solr and a complex (react based) UI we have many dependencies which are hard 

- to track. We need to make sure newer versions of Solr and UI components are supported and tested.
- to test. We have low level network tests side-by-side with UI tests.
- for new people to start with. You have to understand Plone, Solr and React based UIs
- Searches are mostly custom tailerd. It will we hard to maintain a one-size-fits-all-approach.

I suggest to focus on a stable API and do the following for the next major release:

  1. Remove the complex React based search UI and put it in a seperate product. I myself don't use it because I work with eea.facetednavigation and there might be other scenarios.
  2. (Re)add a simple BrowserView based search form using the product with some (faceting) enhancements. This is far more easier to test across diferent Plone versions!
 3. Document how to override Plones search UI with a custom one in Plone 4.3 and Plone 5.

Any feedback? @tisto @gforcada

issue_title: service should mark as finished in-progress tasks
Timeout error message should be a bytes string
issue_body:
At https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/master/git/cmd.py#L650, I think the error message about timeouts should be a `b"..."` bytes string and not a regular string. You can see right afterwards in the endswith call that the string is expected to be a bytes string.

````
                        stderr_value = 'Timeout: the command "%s" did not complete in %d ' \
                                       'secs.' % (" ".join(command), kill_after_timeout)
                ....
                if stderr_value.endswith(b"\n"):
                    stderr_value = stderr_value[:-1]
````

So it should be:

````
                        stderr_value = b'Timeout: the command "%s" did not complete in %d ' \
                                       b'secs.' % (" ".join(command), kill_after_timeout)
````

issue_title: Sorting in TreeModifier.set_done() seems to be inconsistent with git's own sorting -> breaks repository
issue_body:
git enforces tree objects to be sorted by name. GitPython enables users to sort their tree's by calling TreeModifier.set_done(), see:
https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/master/git/objects/tree.py#L45

This sorting somehow seems to be inconsistent with git's own sorting mechanism, which means adding a tree might break the git repository. This means "git fsck" will throw errors and some remotes (gitlab for example) will no accept the commit. Anyways this bug leaves the git object store in an broken state which cannot be fixed easily, especially if there is much history after the broken commit/tree. I had to delete multiple branches from projects I manage because of their broken state so far.

About the problem itself:

GitPython uses core Python functions to sort the file list. This looks correct, but has one major difference to what git itself does. If you have two files beginning with the same chars (for example "file" and "file.second") their ordering will differ:

What git does:
1) "file.second"
2) "file"

What GitPython does:
1) "file"
2) "file.second"

We found this problem using fabdeploit for deployments using git, which allows us to add/removed files to/from the git repository. This way tree's will be modified which means sorting is essential.

Please fix this. ;-)

issue_title: Special-cases in `render_annotations` are not recursively applied in generics
issue_body:
In our `render_annotations` function, we defer to the `__repr__` methods in the `typing` module for rendering generic types in annotations. But this means that the _other_ special cases in `render_annotations` are not applied to types within a generic. For a simple example, consider the type `Dict[str, Optional[int]`. We will render this as `Dict[str, Union[int, NoneType]]` instead, because neither our special case for `Optional` nor our special case for `NoneType -> None` will be applied within the `Dict`.

Some of our other special cases (e.g. for `NewType` or for `builtins`) are also implemented in some form by the `typing` module itself, so they don't appear to break inside a generic.

But basically we have two versions of type-repr, one inside `typing`, and one in our own `render_annotations`; the latter applies only to top level types, the former to everything inside a generic. So we can't add new cases to `render_annotations` and expect them to apply universally. These should be unified in some form, though I'm not sure exactly how to do so; we might need to do our own recursive-descent rendering for generics (though there's probably some private methods/functions in `typing` we can use to avoid reimplementing too much).

issue_title: pyannotate won't annotate methods with args-only keywords
issue_body:
Repro: Modify `gcd.py` in the example directory to the following:

```python
def main():
    print(gcd(15, b=10))
    print(gcd(45, b=12))

def gcd(a, *, b):
    while b:
        a, b = b, a%b
    return a
```

and run

```bash
$ python driver.py
5
3
$ pyannotate -w gcd.py
No files need to be modified.
Warnings/messages while refactoring:
### In file gcd.py ###
gcd.py:6: source has 2 args, annotation has 3 -- skipping
```

issue_title: 0.3 release
issue_body: Featuring new commands compatible with fio-cat and similar programs (#36).

issue_title: Add splash screen message when access token is not valid
issue_body: If a user were to have an invalid access token (token deleted or Mapbox account expired) on their visual, we should present a relevant splash screen message.  That way a user of the visual understands a) who to contact and b) what to do in the case that a visual has an invalid token.

issue_title: Add PIPENV_SKIP_LOCK to disable locking
issue_body:
Locking can be very slow on some machines - for example, I always run pipenv with the `--skip-lock` option.

It would be useful to control that behaviour from an environment variable.

issue_title: Separate testing dependencies from development dependencies
issue_body:
We need another section to specify test only dependencies so that during CI we won't have to install all the development tools as well.
UX-wise we have two options:
The first option is that `pipenv install --dev` will install both development and test dependencies.
To avoid installing test dependencies we can introduce `pipenv install --dev --no-test`.
The second option is to mandate the user to specify `--dev --test` if they want to install both development and test dependencies.

issue_title: When to add initial flags to subsequent calls
issue_body:
I opened a PR #1193 because I tried to update a project's dependencies using the --clear flag, but that flag isn't currently passed to the subsequent `do_lock` function, and is only used on dry-run calls. While digging, I noticed that the verbose flag isn't passed as well.

So this issue's purpose is to ask, what flags should be "passed around" and when?

issue_title: CXXABI symbol version in policy for manylinux is wrong
issue_body:
I believe there is a typo in the policy for the CXXABI symbol version. [While the file lists "3.4.8"](https://github.com/pypa/auditwheel/blob/72f7b94488eb7ac52ffd21ebb223922284752864/auditwheel/policy/policy.json#L11) as the max permissible version, I dumped all the `.gnu.version_d` info from all the whitelisted libs inside the manylinux docker image and found that the max version was actually "1.3.1".

Even on a Debian jessie image (much more recent than the CentOS 5 manylinux image), I cannot find a CXXABI symbol with a version greater than 1.3.8.

issue_title: Flag to remove dependency constraints
issue_body:
Often developers pin their dependencies to exact versions. This is bad practice because it can prevent users from using dependencies that have had security/minor updates. Typically there's also no need to pin versions.

It would be very useful if setuptools had a flag that could be passed to `bdist_wheel` and such so that it would ignore the constraints and just permit any version of the dependency.

issue_title: Migrate to Github
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **jaraco (Bitbucket: [jaraco](http://bitbucket.org/jaraco), GitHub: [jaraco](http://github.com/jaraco))**

----------------------------------------

@RonnyPfannschmidt and others have on more than one occasion asked about migrating from Bitbucket to Github. I'm creating this ticket to track that proposal and execution for Setuptools.

First, I'm generally in support of migration to Github. The current two-repo system (for supporting Travis-based CI and Github contributions) is clumsy at best, and the dominance of Github is undeniable. When Mercurial was chosen for Setuptools, the choice was made to align better with the existing Distribute repo, easier transition for SVN users, and for some of the same reasons that Mercurial was chosen for Python itself. This rationale has minimal value moving forward.

I've previously migrated a couple of projects from Bitbucket to Github including [keyring](https://github.com/jaraco/keyring) and [setuptools_scm](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools_scm). Here's the technique I used:

1. Using late versions of Mercurial, Dulwich, and hg-git, create the Git clone of the Hg repository. Verify that heads, branches, etc, are all represented properly.
2. From Github, request a temporary exemption to the rate throttling on the API, as anything more than 10 or so issues/comments will hit the rate limits.
3. Use [bitbucket_issue_migration](https://github.com/jaraco/bitbucket_issue_migration) to migrate the issues. If one migrates the issues to a clean repository, the new issues will have the same numbering.
4. Disable issue tracking on the original repo.
5. Update references and links in the new and old repositories to direct users as appropriate. Cut a new release to publish these references with the package.

While this process has been adequate for smaller projects, there are some issues that I suspect cannot be simply ignored in migrating larger projects like Setuptools.

## issue attribution and timestamps ##

My biggest concern is about issue attribution and timestamps. The migration is not lossless. Every issue and comment gets a current timestamp and is attributed to the user under which the migration runs. The migration works around this by adding a timestamp and note of original attribution into the body of the text. This makes reviewing of these tickets harder to comprehend. This degradation of quality is acceptable for trivial repositories, but will be substantially less acceptable for a project like Setuptools with hundreds of open and closed tickets. Can this be improved?

## open issues and pull requests remain in the old project ##

With hundreds of open tickets in the old project, users will be subscribed to those tickets and not to the new ones. It will take a great deal of effort to get those tickets closed and updated to refer to the migrated copy. Can this be done automatically (reference the migrated ticket, close it if not closed already, and finally disable the issues on the old repo)?

## closed, anonymous heads ##

Mercurial allows for closed, anonymous heads in the repository. These heads will likely be omitted from the history when pushing to a Git repo. Perhaps that's acceptable, but it will mean a loss of history.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/422

issue_title: Using C extensions in virtualenv with different version to system python causes "Fatal Python Error"
issue_body:
Python 3.6.2rc2, Windows 7:
Upon creation of a virtual environment, the DLLs python3.dll and python3_d.dll aren't copied from the Python installation's root folder. This breaks modules which link against python3.dll when the python on the system PATH is a different version to that inside the virtualenv. For instance, using PyQt5 inside a Python3.5 environment, with Python3.6 on the path, causes a "Fatal Python error: PyThreadState_Get: no current thread"

Steps to recreate issue:
1. Create a virtualenv using a Python version different to the python on the system PATH.
    * Note that python3.dll and python3_d.dll are absent, but python35.dll and python35_d.dll are present.
2. Install PyQt5 inside the virtualenv.
3. Run a Python interpreter inside the environment and attempt to `from PyQt5 import QtCore`
4. Observe python crashes.

issue_title: GridFS ensure indexes is done without yielding
issue_body:
https://github.com/twisted/txmongo/blob/master/txmongo/_gridfs/__init__.py#L55

When instanciating a `GridFS` object, an async call to mongo is done to create the chunks collection indexes.
The trouble is this call is never waited so this can lead to errors if the database is disconnected at the wrong time. This is especially true for tests where a lot of conections/disconnections are done in a short time (as a matter of fact I'm surprised this error didn't show up on travis continuous integration...):

```
(py35-twtrunk)➜  txmongo git:(issue-160) ✗ ./.tox/py35-twtrunk/bin/trial  tests.test_objects.TestGridFsObjects.test_GridFsObjects
tests.test_objects
  TestGridFsObjects
    test_GridFsObjects ...                                              [ERROR]

===============================================================================
[ERROR]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/internet/defer.py", line 1126, in _inlineCallbacks
    result = result.throwExceptionIntoGenerator(g)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/python/failure.py", line 389, in throwExceptionIntoGenerator
    return g.throw(self.type, self.value, self.tb)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/txmongo/collection.py", line 704, in create_index
    yield self._database.system.indexes.insert(index, safe=True, **kwargs)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/internet/defer.py", line 1126, in _inlineCallbacks
    result = result.throwExceptionIntoGenerator(g)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/python/failure.py", line 389, in throwExceptionIntoGenerator
    return g.throw(self.type, self.value, self.tb)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/txmongo/utils/__init__.py", line 27, in _timeout
    raw_result = yield raw_d
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/internet/defer.py", line 1126, in _inlineCallbacks
    result = result.throwExceptionIntoGenerator(g)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/python/failure.py", line 389, in throwExceptionIntoGenerator
    return g.throw(self.type, self.value, self.tb)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/txmongo/collection.py", line 357, in insert
    yield proto.get_last_error(str(self._database), **write_concern.document)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/internet/defer.py", line 1126, in _inlineCallbacks
    result = result.throwExceptionIntoGenerator(g)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/.tox/py35-twtrunk/lib/python3.5/site-packages/twisted/python/failure.py", line 389, in throwExceptionIntoGenerator
    return g.throw(self.type, self.value, self.tb)
  File "/home/emmanuel/projects/txmongo/txmongo/protocol.py", line 391, in get_last_error
    reply = yield self.send_QUERY(query)
pymongo.errors.AutoReconnect: TxMongo lost connection to MongoDB.

tests.test_objects.TestGridFsObjects.test_GridFsObjects
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 1 tests in 0.016s

FAILED (errors=1)
```

One solution I see to solve this would be to store a defer on the ensure_indexes call in the GridFS

```python
>>> gfs = GridFS(db)
>>> # playing with gridfs
>>> yield gfs.ensure_indexes_defer
>>> db.disconnect()
```

Another smaller concern is on the indexes themselves, according to [mongodb's documentation](https://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/gridfs/#gridfs-indexes):
> GridFS uses indexes on each of the chunks and files collections for efficiency. Drivers that conform to the GridFS specification automatically create these indexes for convenience. You can also create any additional indexes as desired to suit your application’s needs.

So I think txmongo should create indexes for the `fs.files` collection as well

issue_title: PickleFifoDiskQueue and FifoMemoryQueue throw EOF when used to persist a crawl
issue_body:
AUTHOR: @Varriount

When using scrapy's PickleFifoDiskQueue and FifoMemoryQueue objects for persistence and request scheduling, an EOF exception is thrown:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\commands\crawl.py", line 58, in run

  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\crawler.py", line 253, in start

  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\twisted\internet\base.py", line 1194, in run
    self.mainLoop()
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\twisted\internet\base.py", line 1203, in mainLoop
    self.runUntilCurrent()
--- <exception caught here> ---
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\twisted\internet\base.py", line 825, in runUntilCurrent
    call.func(*call.args, **call.kw)
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\utils\reactor.py", line 41, in __call__
    return self._func(*self._a, **self._kw)
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\core\engine.py", line 105, in _next_request
    if not self._next_request_from_scheduler(spider):
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\core\engine.py", line 132, in _next_request_from_scheduler
    request = slot.scheduler.next_request()
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\core\scheduler.py", line 68, in next_request
    request = self._dqpop()
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\core\scheduler.py", line 98, in _dqpop
    d = self.dqs.pop()
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\queuelib\pqueue.py", line 43, in pop
    m = q.pop()
  File "C:\x64\python27\lib\site-packages\scrapy-1.1.0dev1-py2.7.egg\scrapy\squeues.py", line 21, in pop
    return deserialize(s)
exceptions.EOFError:
```

There isn't any problem when not persisting the crawl (omitting '-s JOBDIR=crawl-1'), so my best guess is that the problem lies mainly with  PickleFifoDiskQueue.
I'm running Python 2.7 x64 on Windows 8 x64, using the latest Scrapy from the master branch. This bug affects the latest stable Scrapy build as well.

issue_title: auto_offset_reset=-1 not respected for new consumer groups
issue_body:
I noticed that new consumer groups don't start consuming from the end of a topic even if ```auto_offset_reset=-1``` (the default), unless I also set ```reset_offset_on_start=True```.  However, if I set ```reset_offset_on_start=True```, existent consumer groups will always consume from the end of the topic, even if they have committed offsets.

I tested 4 different combinations of consumer groups and ```reset_offset_on_start```.  For all cases below, ```auto_offset_reset=-1``` (the default).  Here's what I found:

- New consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=False```: starts consuming at earliest offset.
- New consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=True```: starts consuming at latest offset.
- Existent consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=False```: starts consuming at committed offset.
- Existent consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=True```: starts consuming at latest offset.

I'm running Kafka 0.8.2.1 and pykafka 2.0.0.

This is a pretty annoying bug, as it means there is no simple way to have a consumer that starts consuming from latest offset UNLESS there is a committed offset, which I believe is the intended behavior of ```auto_offset_reset=-1```.

What should happen with ```auto_offset_reset=-1``` and  ```reset_offset_on_start=False```:

- New consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=False```: starts consuming at **latest** offset.
- Existent consumer group, ```reset_offset_on_start=False```: starts consuming at committed offset.


Thanks!

issue_title: Rename python files to follow python convention names (snake_case)
issue_body:
`snake_case` is the default for python projects, but there're some names in non-compliance to this convention (files in `/nupic`, `/examples`, and `/tests` folders). Examples:

Using UpperCamelCase:
`nupic / nupic / algorithms / CLAClassifier.py`
`nupic / nupic / research / TrivialPredictor.py`

Missing `_` for separate words:
`nupic / nupic / data / functionsource.py`
`nupic / nupic / data / datasethelpers.py`

Unknow achronyms:
`nupic / nupic / data / MCMC.py`

These and other files names should be fixed to `snake_case` convention and if possible use more descriptive names.

issue_title: WebComment: introduce separate Comments (N1) and Reviews (N2) tabs
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-08-31

Invenio offers optional commenting and reviewing facilities that every
installation can separately enable or disable.  (This is governed by
`CFG_WEBCOMMENT_ALLOW_COMMENTS` and `CFG_WEBCOMMENT_ALLOW_REVIEWS`.)

However, on the HTML detailed format on the Detailed record pages,
there is only one common tab called "Discussion (3)" where comments
and reviews are presented as sub-tabs, so to speak.  This does not
work coherently with the HTML brief format where we display comment
and review information apart:

```
1. Development of photon beam diagnostics for VUV radiation from a
   SASE FEL / Treusch, R ; Lokajczyk, T ; Xu, W ; Jastrow, U ; et al
   [TESLA-FEL-99-07]

   Detailed record - Similar records - 2 comments - 1 review
```

The goal of this ticket is to introduce two separate "Comments (2)"
and "Reviews (1)" tabs on the detailed record pages, which will both
harmonise the situation and make the UI clearer should a site enable
one or both of these facilities.

issue_title: WebSearch: new second-pass search operator
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-12-05

It would be useful to have a second-pass search operator, say `@`, so that power users could write queries like:

```
author:ellis@affiliation:CERN
author:ellis@relator:editor
title:"neutrino mixing"@provenance:arXiv
```

This would work in a general second-pass manner: firstly, retrieve results as you do now (including false positives); secondly, pass through results calling field instance filtering function that would pick only the fields having wanted another subfield.  See also #1550.

The price to pay for a very generic `@` second-pass search operator would be the slowness in case the first step returns say 500K results through which the second step would have to crawl in order to filter out only the interesting gems that the user wanted.  The post-filtering step could be very slow then.

This could be speeded up further for regular search needs, such as find-this-author-at-this-institute, via a new kind of index offering search for:

```
author:ellis@cern
```
 
Howeer, this optimisation can be done later, since a generic second-pass search operator would be useful to have anyway.

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: api: customizable response header
issue_body:
**Use case**: Storing metadata for each file. One way of doing this is to create a record whenever a file is uploaded, and somehow give the link to the record in the upload response.

**Who needs this**: B2Share

**Problem**: invenio-files-rest tries to be compatible with the Amazon S3 API. Thus we cannot modify the response content as it might conflict with an existing definition provided by Amazon.

**Suggested solution**: Enable the customization of response header.

**How it would be used**: [rfc5988](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988) enables to return links in the HTTP response header. The link pointing to a file's metadata could be typed as [describedby](http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).
There is few chances that this will conflict with Amazon S3 API, and if it does, it would still be possible to define a proprietary link type. It is up to the overlay to make sure that there is no conflict.

issue_title: Don't save configs generated by the forker.  They just end up being annoying.
No session.rollback() when transaction.abort() is called.
issue_body:
When the default setting of `keep_session=False` is used this isn't an issue, because the connection pool will reset the connection before it is used again.  It seems like the session would need to be rolled back when `keep_session=True` is used.

I see that the two phase data manager issues a rollback in `tpc_abort()`.

Is this (`keep_session=True, twophase=True`) just a configuration that nobody uses (perhaps for some reason I've overlooked)?

issue_title: Add support for swappable token model
Run callback in a new thread
issue_body:
In pymesos' current implementation, callback  bocks the `io_thread`, this is a big problem in some case.
such as

    def reregistered(self, driver, masterInfo):
        self.reconcile_all_running_tasks()
        with self.lock:
            self._connected = True

`reconcile_all_running_tasks` will wait util it received statusUpdate of these tasks, but because it blocks the `io_thread`, so the `statusUpdate` doesn't have a chance to run, finally it becomes a dead  loop.

issue_title: compute forward returns using datetime offset
issue_body:
Currently, It requires a lot of work for `compute_forward_returns` to compute the correct timedelta. As discussed in [issue 253](https://github.com/quantopian/alphalens/issues/253#issuecomment-367131795), the problem is that returns are computed in terms of row location offset instead of the actual datetime offset.

If datetime offsets can be directly passed to `compute_forward_returns`, the function can be simplified a lot. Returns can be computed as  `pct_change(period, freq=freq)` and there's no need to infer the trading calendar. Also, `compute_forward_returns` can completely decouple from factor, so that the computed returns can be reused with other factors.

issue_title: Implement patronictl detach
issue_body: Currently, on exit Patroni tries to shut down the PostgreSQL database it manages. This makes sense generally (otherwise we won't be able to guarantee that the leftover database will keep its role), but on some occasions (i.e. upgrading Patroni itself) we might want to detach Patroni from the PostgreSQL cluster and keep the latter running.

issue_title: implement patroni-cli
issue_body:
In order to implement a number of features for patroni, among them issue #11, we need an actual UI for dynamic adminstration of Patroni systems.  I anticipate having both a CLI and a web UI.  I plan to start with the CLI.

The CLI will be a python program which installs with Patroni.  It will have the following spec:

* must install all patroni libraries
* have a configuration file, patroni-cli.yml, which contains connection information to the DCS
* might be a standard CLI, or might be a full-on command shell.  Opinions?

Call: patroni-cli --config config-file --cluster clustername command [options]

--config is not optional, and contains connection information for the DCS, as well as potentially auth information for the APIs.  Possibly it should be a pure positional argument.

--cluster is optional, and is there mainly for shops which have multiple clusters.  For teams with only one cluster, the clustername could be contained in the config file instead.  The command line paramter supercedes clustername in the config file.

Initially, I expect to implement the following commands:

**status**: prints out cluster status

**status $node**: prints detailed information on the status of a particular node

**list**: lists all nodes

**shutdown $node**: shuts down the named node

**restart $node**: makes node restart

**failover**: initates failover by having the master release the key and reboot read-only

**failover $node**: initiates manual failover per above, but to the named node

**disable $ttl**: disables auto-failover for the whole cluster, with optional TTL 

**resume**: resumes auto-failover for the cluster

**initialize $node**: makes node re-initialize itself

issue_title: support piping config to patroni
issue_body:
In order for Patroni to work better on Kubernetes/Docker, it would be very useful if we could pass the Patroni configuration into new containers as one big variable instead of needing to have a shell script or CMS setup which writes a config file.  This would require:

1. support JSON for config as well as YAML (should be trivial)
2. support config as single value with the --config switch

Doesn't seem more complicated than that; any gotchas before I start working on this?

issue_title: style_map components should be given data on render
issue_body:
At the moment, `style_map` components do not receive any data beyond the text to style (as `props['children]`).

https://github.com/springload/draftjs_exporter/blob/209631a107be74837b1be2f7c0697f77d64d994b/draftjs_exporter/style_state.py#L26-L32

This is ok for common use cases (`BOLD`, `ITALIC`, etc), but it makes the style_map fallback rather useless – there is no way to know what style needs the fallback, or have any other information about the context to adjust the fallback behavior.

Here's what the `block_map` fallback has access to for comparison:

```py
props['block'] = {
        'type': type_,
        'depth': depth,
        'data': data,
}
```

In retrospect I think this could've been all of the block's attributes, not just a cherry-picked shortlist, so for inline styles we could pass the following exhaustive `props`:

```py
{
    # The style range to render.
    "range": 
        "offset": 10,
        "length": 17,
        "style": "BOLD"
    },
    # The full block data, eg.
    "block": {
            "key": "t7k7",
            "text": "Unstyled test test test test test",
            "type": "unstyled",
            "depth": 0,
            "inlineStyleRanges": [
                {
                    "offset": 10,
                    "length": 17,
                    "style": "BOLD"
                }
            ],
            "entityRanges": [
                {
                    "offset": 0,
                    "length": 4,
                    "key": 6
                }
            ],
            "data": {}
        },
}
```

Here's the approximative change:

```diff
-    def render_styles(self, text_node):
+    def render_styles(self, text_node, block):
        node = text_node
        if not self.is_empty():
            # Nest the tags.
            for s in sorted(self.styles, reverse=True):
                opt = Options.for_style(self.style_map, s)
+                props['block'] = block
+                props['range'] = s
                node = DOM.create_element(opt.element, opt.props, node)

        return node
```

---

Ideally I'd like entities and blocks to also be given more data (enough data to recreate the whole ContentState, thus making the exporter usable to create content migrations), but that's a separate issue.

issue_title: Importer les barèmes IPP
issue_body:
Les [barèmes IPP](http://www.ipp.eu/outils/baremes-ipp/) sont une source importante des paramètres d'OpenFisca-France aujourd'hui. L'import en est en partie automatisé, mais selon un processus éclaté dans plusieurs dépôts.

issue_title: Different results between calculate and simulate endpoints
issue_body:
There is a difference between `calculate` and `simulate` endpoints for the same test case.

The next step to solve this issue is to develop a [decomposition](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/next/openfisca_france/decompositions/decomp.xml) checker which guarantees that the sums of the aggregated nodes equals the calculated variable with the same name.

For exemple the [`psoc`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/a3772aef8b5e58876205b6c0bf30b1fcb888c06b/openfisca_france/decompositions/decomp.xml#L83-L116) node aggregates 3 sub-nodes (`pfam`, `mini` and `aides_logement`), and the variable [`psoc`](http://legislation.openfisca.fr/variables/psoc) sums the same 3 variables.

That's correct but this could have been wrong if `decomp.xml` was false.

```
Exception ignored in: <bound method SingleInstance.__del__ of <tendo.singleton.SingleInstance object at 0x7faf9af8bf60>>

2.26user 0.51system 1:31.64elapsed 3%CPU (0avgtext+0avgdata 27284maxresident)k
18944inputs+936outputs (61major+9800minor)pagefaults 0swaps

Remove these 2 lines:

~~import sys~~
~~import os~~

from

```python
  def __del__(self):
        import sys
        import os
        if not self.initialized:

        try:

issue_title: Lookup parameter is updated after set_updated(False)
issue_body:
Calling `set_updated(False)` on a parameter should cause that parameter not to be updated, but it seems to be ignored. Am I using it incorrectly?

Minimal working example:
```
import dynet_config
dynet_config.set(weight_decay=0)
import dynet as dy
import numpy as np
from numpy.testing import assert_array_equal

m = dy.ParameterCollection()
p = m.add_lookup_parameters((2, 2))
p.set_updated(False)
init = np.array(((2, 3), (4, 5)), dtype=float)
p.init_from_array(init)
t = dy.SimpleSGDTrainer(m)
e = dy.sum_elems(p.expr())
e.forward()
e.backward()
t.update()
dy.renew_cg()
assert_array_equal(p.as_array(), init)
# >>> AssertionError: 
# Arrays are not equal
#
# (mismatch 100.0%)
#  x: array([[1.9, 2.9],
#        [3.9, 4.9]])
#  y: array([[2., 3.],
#        [4., 5.]])
```
Calling `expr(update=False)` fixes the problem, but I think `set_updated` should be enough.

issue_title: Floating point bug in logistic function
issue_body:
When the input to the function `logistic(x) = 1 / (1 + exp(-x))` is large and negative (say below -100), `exp(-x)` will overflow and the resulting value should be 0. However, I'm instead getting the following results.

MacBook Pro running OS X El Capitan:
```
>>> dy.logistic(dy.scalarInput(-100)).value()
0.0
>>> dy.logistic(dy.inputVector([-100] + [0] * 6)).npvalue()
array([ 0. ,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5])
>>> dy.logistic(dy.inputVector([-100] + [0] * 7)).npvalue()
array([ 0.00990099,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,
        0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997])
```

EC2 c4.4xlarge instance, default Ubuntu AMI (Ubuntu Server 16.04 LTS (HVM), SSD Volume Type - ami-b7a114d7):
```
>>> dy.logistic(dy.scalarInput(-100)).value()
0.0
>>> dy.logistic(dy.inputVector([-100] + [0] * 6)).npvalue()
array([ 0. ,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  0.5])
>>> dy.logistic(dy.inputVector([-100] + [0] * 7)).npvalue()
array([        nan,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997,
        0.49999997,  0.49999997,  0.49999997])
```

In particular, I'm getting a `nan` on Linux for vectors of length 8 or greater. Comparable C++ tests give the same results, as do MKL and non-MKL installations of DyNet.

This bug has appeared in an organic setting during several long-running experiments involving LSTMs or other RNNs that use the `logistic` function for gating. The `nan` value immediately propagates to the other parameters through the trainer and causes a hard failure.

Any suggestions or workarounds would be much appreciated!

issue_title: Antenna transitions not used in dealiasing routines
issue_body: The antenna_transition* attribute of the **Radar** object is not used in the dealiasing algorithms (or other algorithms) in Py-ART.  This means that rays in which the antenna is in transitions between sweeps are treated like collected sweeps in these algorithms. These ray should be ignored by default and not included in the correction.  One possible solution would be to add an `exclude_transition` method to the **Gatefilter** class and have all gates in rays which are marked as in transition be excluded by default by the `moment_based_gate_filter` function.

issue_title: Allow any languages under DFLT script
issue_body:
quoting the updated OpenType spec https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/typography/opentype/spec/chapter2

> a font is permitted to have a 'DFLT' script table with non-default language system tables, and an application may use features associated with one of these if the the 'DFLT' script table is applicable — no script table is present for the specific script, or there is no specific script in the text context — and if one of the particular language systems is specified. Under such conditions, applications should support use of a non-default language system table that is associated with 'DFLT' script if a font includes tables with this configuration.

but the published version of the Adobe Feature File spec still says:

> The only permitted language tag for the 'DFLT script is 'dflt'.

The latter should be changed to match the OpenType spec.

Also see https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/pull/1278

issue_title: makeotf should probably not re-order ligature carets
issue_body:
In makeotf v2.0.90, both this input:
```
table GDEF {
  LigatureCaretByPos f_f_i 200;
  LigatureCaretByIndex f_f_i 8;
} GDEF;
```

and this input:
```
table GDEF {
  LigatureCaretByIndex f_f_i 8;
  LigatureCaretByPos f_f_i 200;
} GDEF;
```

give the following output:
```xml
  <GDEF>
    <Version value="1.0"/>
    <LigCaretList>
      <Coverage>
        <Glyph value="f_f_i"/>
      </Coverage>
      <!-- LigGlyphCount=1 -->
      <LigGlyph index="0">
        <!-- CaretCount=2 -->
        <CaretValue index="0" Format="1">
          <Coordinate value="200"/>
        </CaretValue>
        <CaretValue index="1" Format="2">
          <CaretValuePoint value="8"/>
        </CaretValue>
      </LigGlyph>
    </LigCaretList>
  </GDEF>
```

So, makeotf seems to always put absolute coordinates (LigatureCaretByPos) before contour points (LigatureCaredByIndex), even if the feature file specifies a different order. Doesn’t this re-ordering change the semantics?

issue_title: Error fetching results from a BigQuery asynchronous call in 0.19.0
issue_body:
Referring to the [doc](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/stable/bigquery-usage.html#querying-data-asynchronous), calling `QueryResults.fetch_data` raises an exception since `self.name` is `None`:

```python
if self.name is None:
    raise ValueError("Query not yet executed:  call 'run()'")
```

However, if `QueryResults.run` is called, an exception is also raised since `self._job` already exists:

```python
if self._job is not None:
    raise ValueError("Query job is already running.")
```

issue_title: WRITE_TRUNCATE appending to table
issue_body:
Using the templates found [here](http://gcloud-python.readthedocs.io/en/stable/bigquery-table.html) 
Running the commands
```
job = client.load_table_from_storage('load-from-storage'+str(datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M")),
                                             table, gsbucket)
job.skip_leading_rows = 0
job.writeDisposition = 'WRITE_TRUNCATE'
job.field_delimiter = ','
job.begin()
```
This piece of code appends to my table instead of overwriting it like the [docs](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs) say.
> WRITE_TRUNCATE: If the table already exists, BigQuery overwrites the table data. 
I know this becuase if I use:
```
table.reload()
print table.num_rows
```
after the job it has increased by 2 million - the size of the table.

I use a workaround as follows:
```
if disposition=="WRITE_TRUNCATE":
    schema = table.schema
    while table.exists():
        table.delete()
    table = dataset.table(table_name, schema=schema)
    table.create()
```
Which seems to work fine, and the while loop is just me making certain that the table is deleted but when I tested it without the while loop it also worked.

Anyway, the issue is WRITE_TRUNCATE isn't doing what it say it does in the docs.

issue_title: Cloudwatch Alarms support a parameter in the Threshold property, yet troposphere requires a basestring
Not possible to use parameters of type `List` with VpcConfig
issue_body:
`VpcConfig` is defined in Troposphere as:

```
class VPCConfig(AWSProperty):

    props = {
        'SecurityGroupIds': (list, True),
        'SubnetIds': (list, True),
    }
```

The problem is that using `list` as Python data type for `SecurityGroupIds` and `SubnetIds` prevents using parameters with type `List` (e.g. `List<AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id>`).

Sample:
```
subnets = template.add_parameter(Parameter(
    "ParamMySubnets",
    Type="List<AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id>",
    Description='My VPC subnets'
)

template..add_resource(Function(
    'MyLambda',
    VpcConfig=VPCConfig(
        SubnetIds=Ref(subnets),  # <===== doesn't work with Troposphere
    )
))
```

My current workaround is to overwrite the VpcConfig class and using `basestring` as the Python data type for `SecurityGroupIds` and `SubnetIds`.

It would be great to change VpcConfig in order to be able to use both approaches with an array and also with a single parameter of type List.

issue_title: Inline execution of Dependencies/Projection Node
issue_body:
Currently, DependenciesNode and ProjectionNode are always created and stored in the ProductGraph. This has the effect of memoizing/caching their execution, and 99% of the time this is overkill. It helps to have the Nodes in the graph for debugging purposes, but we're not interested in caching or threading for them, let alone multiprocessing.

A simple transform to not actually create Nodes that we won't cache/memoize involves recursively inlining the `step` method on non-task/non-FS dependency Nodes. But there is a question of whether (since in those cases they're never persisted in the ProductGraph) the intermediate Nodes should even be Node subclasses.

issue_title: Request for Comments: Automatic Alarms for resources declared in SAM template
issue_body:
### What are you proposing?

SAM helps simplify the definition of Lambda functions, API Gateway and associated configurations. But it does not provide yet any mechanisms to simplify operational aspects of your application. I am proposing adding the ability for SAM to automatically create Cloudwatch alarms for all resources in the template.

### Example code
```yaml
Globals:
  Function:
    Alarms:
      # All Lambda functions will get a Throttle CloudWatch alarm. If necessary, each function can override certain properties alarm as they need
       Throttles:
          Metric: "throttle"
          # Notify if the Lambda gets throttled in 5min interval
          Threshold: 1
          Timeframe: "5min"
          Notification: 
             Type: Email
             Address: pager@example.com 

MyFunction:
  Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
  Properties:
      Runtime: nodejs4.3
      CodeUri: s3://bucket/key
      ...
     Alarms:
       Throttles:
             # Override the timeframe to 10mins for this function
            Timeframe: "10min"
       Errors:
          Metric: "my-custom-metric"
          Threshold: 100
          Timeframe: "1min"
          Notification: 
             Type: SNS
             Address: !Ref OpsSnsTopic
```

Alarms will also be available for API resources as well. It is just not shown in the above template.

### Automatic alarms
In addition to the above syntax, I propose adding a short-cut for example, `Alarms: "default"` which is a special case where SAM will automatically create necessary alarms for each resource with reasonable default configuration that should "just work" out of the box. 

"default" will expand to a Alarm Template that is pre-configured in SAM and will be publicly documented. 

In the future, we could expose make Alarm Template functionality a first-class citizen allowing users to define their own alarm templates. So you could define the template once, and use `Alarms: "my-prod-template"` to let SAM automatically create alarms based on your template.  

### How would this work?
When an Alarms configuration is specified, SAM will automatically create `AWS::CloudWatch::Metric` for the specific resource and `AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm` on the metric with given configuration. Users will be able to refer to the generated Metric & Alarm resources in rest of the template using `!Ref MyFunction.Metric.<metric-name>` or `!Ref MyFunction.Alarm.<alarm-name>` shorthand syntax that SAM will provide.

### Process
This Issue describes a very broad set of features. We need your feedback to understand what is important to you. Please comment with your thoughts and help us evolve the requirements into a spec that can be implemented.

- [ ] **Request for comments**: In Progress
- [ ] **Spec design**
- [ ] **Acceptance**

issue_title: Support sharing registry across multiple workers (where possible)
issue_body:
Similar to https://github.com/prometheus/client_ruby/issues/9, when using the python client library on workers that get load balanced by something like gunicorn or uwsgi, each scrape it hits only one worker since they can't share state with others.

At least uwsgi supports sharing memory: http://uwsgi-docs.readthedocs.org/en/latest/SharedArea.html
This should be used to share the registry across all workers. Maybe gunicorn supports something similar.

issue_title: Add stemmer generators for C# code
issue_body:
Hi there,

I would like to know if anyone is interested on having support for C# generators in this project. Generating C# code is actually quite different from what the Java generator does, as those languages, albeit similar, don't have the same constructs (i.e. labelled breaks/loops). As such, in order to create a generator for C#, it would be necessary to mix parts from the C/C++ generator and the Java one. Plus, it would also be interesting to have a .NET library for Snowball, in the same spirit as the Java one that is available in this repository.

As such, I just wanted to add that I have started a new fork to address those issues that is almost completely finished, with support for a new [generator_csharp.c](https://github.com/cesarsouza/snowball/blob/csharp-development/compiler/generator_csharp.c) that can already generate all stemmer definition files that come with Snowball. The generated C# files could be seen, for example, here:

 - https://github.com/cesarsouza/snowball/tree/csharp-development/csharp/Snowball/Algorithms

I have also added some unit tests for some languages, though not all of them yet. So, if any project maintainer would like this feature, I can send a pull request to merge my fork'd version. Otherwise I can just keep my fork around for anyone else that could be interested.

The fork is currently available at [https://github.com/cesarsouza/snowball](https://github.com/cesarsouza/snowball). In order to generate C# files, you can pass "-cs" to the command line compiler, as in

 - snowball.exe stem_ISO_8859_1.sbl -cs -o EnglishStemmer -name EnglishStemmer -u   

An [example generated file can be found here](https://github.com/cesarsouza/snowball/blob/csharp-development/csharp/Snowball/Algorithms/EnglishStemmer.generated.cs).

Hope it can be useful! :smiley:

When Data objects are added to a Run's inputs or outputs with no lsid but a pipelinePath or DataFileUrl you receive the following java Exception from the server:

org.labkey.api.view.NotFoundException: Could not find data with LSID null
        at org.labkey.api.data.ExpDataFileConverter.resolveExpData(ExpDataFileConverter.java:80)

I believe because the code's to_json is added the lsid property to the dict to be serialized even when it is None, then the key exists on the JSONObject server side and then tries to resolve the ExpData object via a null LSID instead of creating a new ExpData from the pipelinePath or DataFileURL.

issue_title: Are we using the wrong file to "glue" together core URLs?
issue_body:
At the top of **kolibri/core/urls.py**:

```Kolibri core url patterns
=========================

This module should be the glue between all other url patterns.

Do NOT use kolibri.deployment.default project's urlconf for such! That file can
be modified by users.
...
```

In **kolibri/deployment/default/urls.py**:

```urlpatterns = [
    url(r'^admin/', include(admin.site.urls)),
    url(r'', include('kolibri.core.urls')),
    url(r'', include('kolibri.core.content.urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.auth.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.content.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.logger.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.tasks.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.exams.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.device.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api/', include('kolibri.core.lessons.api_urls')),
    url(r'^api-auth/', include('rest_framework.urls', namespace='rest_framework')),
    url(r'', include(morango_urls)),
]
...
```

So..... it seems we're not following the pattern we specified? Should we be moving those URLs into kolibri/core/urls.py? I'm trying to remember whether there was some sort of blocker for doing that -- I know I've added stuff into the deployment file, but I think I was just following the pattern we used with other core apps.

issue_title: remove all instances of app logic being triggered by beforeDestroy
issue_body:
proposal:

Vue lifecycle hooks - particularly `beforeDestroy` - should not be used to trigger application logic.

The vue component lifecycle hooks are tied to the DOM lifecycle, not the application lifecycle. `beforeDestroy` should only be used for low-level cleanup operations like removing DOM event listeners, clearing timer, and cleaning up callbacks.

Application that might currently be triggered by `beforeDestroy` should instead be triggered by whatever vuex action or state change actually _caused_ the component to be destroyed.

-----

refs:

* #2034

issue_title: map API data to vuex state
issue_body:
Leftover task from #1257 

Currently when displaying learner/exercise detail reports, data from the server is dropped directly into vuex without an intermediary mapping state which explicitly defines which attributes are extracted and where they're stored. This means that state is implicitly defined in vue files, and we end up with snake_case attributes (e.g. `exercise.extra_fields`).

We should add the appropriate state mappers in coach reports actions.

issue_title: hdx.get_validlocations() network call should not be part of Configuration.create
issue_body:
It would be better if this external network call was an explicit method on the Configuration. Configuration objects should be able to be used in environments without network access. It is also easy to make the mistake of calling Configuration.create many times without realizing that each call results in a network request, resulting in hundreds or more superfluous requests to the CKAN API. 

This is somewhat related to https://github.com/OCHA-DAP/hdx-python-api/issues/7 - if it was explicit that Configuration.create is only called once, and the resulting object was passed around, repeated calls to get_validlocations would be less of an issue

issue_title: Create Anechoic / Free field environment
issue_body:
This would be useful to create anechoic simulations with properly simulated delays between microphones.
There isn't much work required to get there. It is already possible to achieve this result by creating a room (shoebox preferably) with `max_order` = 0. The only constraint then is to place the sources in the room, which is not really required if we think about this in terms of free field environment.
Maybe instead it would be possible to create a room without any walls.

issue_title: HTTPException('got more than 100 headers',)
issue_body: When using Akamai request headers to debug Edge calls, the server will frequently return a large number of debug headers. Unfortunately, the default limit for http.client is 100 headers in the response. We should change that default value in the Akamai EdgeGrid client to 1000.

issue_title: Faster implementation of marginalize
issue_body: Currently marginalize method uses `_marginalize_single_variable` and in the case of marginalizing over multiple variables, it iterates over the variables marginalizing each of them. Improve this implementation so that marginalization over all the variables is done in a single iteration (implementation of maximize method is done is this way).

issue_title: Support building UFOs
issue_body:
README says, 

> This library provides a wrapper for several other Python libraries which together compile fonts from various sources (.glyphs, .ufo) into binaries (.otf, .ttf).

but it seems to only accept .glyphs files atm,

In reading https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/blob/master/Lib/fontmake/font_project.py then I suppose there needs to be a default assumption that an input UFO is a master or an instance, and an arg to declare this I propose that assuming UFOs are masters makes more sense.

issue_title: create_advanced_arb_sequence() always returns 1 coerced marker, more can crash you
issue_body:
create_advanced_arb_sequence() returns an array of coerced_markers_array.
Turns out its size has to be `None if marker_location_array is None or len(marker_location_array)` but right now it's always 1 due to how our code generator works:

We forgot to specify it's size metadata, but in metadata_add_all.py we assign a fixed size of 1. Later, at codegen, we don't question it.

I'm adding an assert at codegen to catch these.

issue_title: incompatibility with recent Twisted trunk
issue_body:
I have a set of unit tests (in https://github.com/warner/magic-wormhole) that recently started failing when I combine autobahn-0.13.1 and Twisted trunk. It looks like https://github.com/twisted/twisted/commit/4f9e36d8ad21f9d73369f4d506bab30f61c2ad71 (landed 29-Mar-2016) is where the problem started (that's the first revision after Twisted-16.1.1 was branched off that causes my project's tests to fail).

The commit says:

    Merge hide-request-transport-8191-5: Hide the transport backing the HTTPChannel object from twisted.web Resource objects.

And my tests are using `WebSocketResource` to put a websocket on a specific URL. I haven't tracked down the specific failure yet, but it seems like the server is having an internal error when the client attempts to connect to it, or the server isn't accepting the connection at all.

The relevant Twisted bug is at https://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/8191 .

When I get some time (maybe next week), I'll figure out how to run autobahn's test suite against various versions of Twisted and see what happens.
