issue_title: Option to ignore columns with falsy headers
issue_body:
# Overview

We'd like to have argument to ignore columns with falsy headers.

# Plan

- [ ] provide such an options for `Stream`
- [ ] ignore columns with falsy headers if option is true
- [ ] tests/readme

---

From _@mcarans wrote_

When reading the below file, the stream.headers contains a None field and the rows read in using `stream.read(keyed=True, limit=chunksize)` contain a None key in each dictionary:

http://www.acleddata.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/07/ACLED-All-Africa-File_20170101-to-20170708.xlsx

Is this correct behaviour and if it is, perhaps there should be an option to ignore headers with None?

Currently I have to do:
```
                for fieldname in stream.headers:
                    if fieldname is not None:
                        schema.append({'id': fieldname, 'type': 'text'})
                    else:
                        nonefieldname = True
...
            rowset = stream.read(keyed=True, limit=chunksize)
            while len(rowset) != 0:
                if nonefieldname:
                    for row in rowset:
                        del row[None]

```

See - https://github.com/frictionlessdata/tabulator-py/issues/180

---

_From @roll_

Current workaround:

```py
from pprint import pprint
from tabulator import Stream

def ignore_columns_with_falsy_headers(extended_rows):
    for number, headers, row in extended_rows:
        cleaned_headers = []
        cleaned_row = []
        for index, header in enumerate(headers):
            if header is not None:
                cleaned_headers.append(header)
                cleaned_row.append(row[index])
        yield (number, cleaned_headers, cleaned_row)

source = 'http://www.acleddata.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/07/ACLED-All-Africa-File_20170101-to-20170708.xlsx'
with Stream(source, headers=1, post_parse=[ignore_columns_with_falsy_headers]) as stream:
    pprint([header for header in stream.headers if header is not None])
    pprint(stream.read(limit=1, keyed=True)
```

issue_title: Ensure dump.to_path|zip saves UTF-8 descriptor (ATM it is ascii)
issue_body:
## the problem

`"title": "мамы"` becomes `"title": "\u043c\u0430\u043c\u044b"` after `dump.to_file`

## the reason

Python's  `json.dump[s]` save json file in `ascii` encoding by default:
https://docs.python.org/3/library/json.html#basic-usage
> `ensure_ascii=True`

So does the DPP - saves descriptors in the `ASCII` despite the file encoding is `utf-8`:
https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/0bc5276c2acd730fd765af99dbfdecbca9b1c46d/datapackage/package.py#L249
https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/0bc5276c2acd730fd765af99dbfdecbca9b1c46d/datapackage/package.py#L269


## how it should be:
Here is a standard of JSON: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7159#section-8.1
> 8.1.  Character Encoding
>   JSON text SHALL be encoded in UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32.  The default
   encoding is UTF-8, and JSON texts that are encoded in UTF-8 are
   interoperable in the sense that they will be read successfully by the
   maximum number of implementations; there are many implementations
   that cannot successfully read texts in other encodings (such as
   UTF-16 and UTF-32).
>   Implementations MUST NOT add a byte order mark to the beginning of a
   JSON text.  In the interests of interoperability, implementations
   that parse JSON texts MAY ignore the presence of a byte order mark
   rather than treating it as an error.


@akariv @roll  Do you have any objections if I fix it ?

issue_title: Work out a design for fetching release notes
issue_body:
We need a way of fetching release notes for all of the releases between the current and upcoming revisions if we want `globus update` to show what's changing.

I've thought about a few ways of doing this, and they all have problems.

Basically, I see 3 sensible places for us to put changelog data for this feature:
- In pypi.org , by adding a changelog to the `long_description` field and ensuring that we can parse it
- In downloads.globus.org , where it can be whatever format is convenient (multiple txt files, single JSON file, etc), e.g. `downloads.globus.org/cli/changelog.json`
- In the CLI repo's `master` branch, where it should probably be a single file (probably JSON to ease parsing)
- docs.globus.org , potentially as a JSON or flat text file alongside `docs.globus.org/cli/reference/changelog`

The options wherein we need the data to be non-json but parseable or recognizable in some way are bound to be fragile. That basically rules out pypi , but not the other options.

I also feel nervous about relying on `https://github.com/globus/globus-cli/raw/master/changelog.json` or somesuch -- if we move the repo or change things around in the future, this seems pretty limiting.
It's unlikely that we'll change hosting or anything in the foreseeable future, but I'd like not to sow problems for us into the future.

docs.globus.org and downloads.globus.org are under our control, and the only downside I can see is
- for docs.globus.org, you need your PR to merge for new docs to be uploaded
- for downloads.globus.org, we'd need to distribute credentials to CLI devs for uploading a new changelog to S3

Both seem fine to me -- and better than trying to build on top of pypi or GitHub -- and then I guess it's a question of what @jaswilli, @shifflett-globus, and @aaschaer prefer?
Or is there some other place we could put our changelog to keep it parseable which I'm missing?

issue_title: Block importing crashes with KeyError when validating uncles
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

When validating a newly imported block, we do a `self.get_vm(uncle)` for every uncle of the block, but that will crash when the uncle has not been previously persisted to the DB. That happens because the `get_vm()` call will try to load the uncle's transactions from the DB and will crash with a KeyError when those are not found. This is a regression that has been introduced by #740 -- before that we didn't have the `self.get_vm(uncle)` call as we were using the imported block's VM to perform the uncle validation

### How can it be fixed

Not sure

issue_title: Decouple blockchain opcodes and state mutable data
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

1. Decouple `VMState.block_header` and `VMState` properties (`timestamp, block_number, difficulty, and gas_limit)`, because shard chains use `period_start_prevhash` block properties of main chain in contracts, but `VMState.state_db()` should use the `state_root` of `VMState.collation_header` to build the state root trie.
2. Note that only `COINBASE` opcode refers to the shard coinbase.


### How can it be fixed

1. As v suggested, implement a simple `BlockInfo` class:

    ```python
    class BlockInfo():
        # For shard chain, refer to the shard coinbase.
        _coinbase = None

        # For shard chian, block data of period_start_prevhash.
        _timestamp = None
        _number = None
        _difficulty = None
        _gas_limit = None
        _prev_headers = None

        def __init__(
                self,
                coinbase,
                timestamp,
                number,
                difficulty,
                gas_limit,
                prev_headers):
            self._coinbase = coinbase
            self._timestamp = timestamp
            self._number = number
            self._difficulty = difficulty
            self._gas_limit = gas_limit
            self._prev_headers = prev_headers

        @property
        def coinbase(self):
            return self._coinbase

        @property
        def timestamp(self):
            return self._timestamp

        @property
        def number(self):
            return self._number

        @property
        def difficulty(self):
            return self._difficulty

        @property
        def gas_limit(self):
            return self._gas_limit

        @property
        def prev_headers(self):
            return self._prev_headers
    ```

2. Pass  `block_info` to initialize `VMState`.
3. Assign `coinbase` separately.
4. Arrange/Refactor the temporary mutable data during state transition: 
	* Part of  the given block’s header from `VMState.__init__(..., block_header, ...)` and replace with  `VMState.apply_transaction(...,block,...)`
		* `state_root`
		* `gas_used` - update in `VMState.make_receipt`
	* `receipts`  - now is part of `VMState`
	* **Solution 1**: generalize `VMState.block_header` to `VMState.header` and make sure it’s compatible for both `BlockHeader` and `CollationHeader` structure.
	* **Solution 2**:
		1. pass `block` to `VMState.make_receipt` function so that we just use the given block to maintain `gas_used`.
		2. pass `state_root` and `receipts` to `VMState.__init__(...,state_root, receipts, ...)`
	* **Solution 3**: apply Vitalik’s suggestion to arrange these variables to a new simple `StateObj` structure. And the apply transaction would become:

    ```python
    # In create_block level function
    # state_obj: previous state_obj or new StateObj

    state_obj = StateObj(
        state_root=state_obj.state_root,
        gas_used=state_obj.gas_used,
        receipts=state_obj.receipts,
    )
    block_info = BlockInfo(
        coinbase=block.header.coinbase,    # or collation.coinbase
        timestamp=block.header.timestamp,
        number=block.header.number,
        difficulty=block.header.difficulty,
        gas_limit=block.header.gas_limit,
        prev_headers=prev_headers,
    )
    vm_state = cls.get_state_class()(
        chaindb=witness_db,
        state_obj=state_obj,
        block_info=block_info,
    )
    computation, result_block, _ = vm_state.apply_transaction(
        transaction=transaction,
        block=block,
        is_stateless=True,
    )
    state_obj = computation.vm_state.state_obj
    ```
    I'd like to try **Solution 2** first because I want keep the `VMState` simple, and I'm also fine with **Solution 3**.

5. Check with @pipermerriam : do we design that `VMState` only be used for handling the “transaction level” state transition? Or we would introduce “block level” function like `VMState.apply_block` function?
 
6. About uncle validation, there are: 
	* `Chain.mine_block` or `Chain.import_block(block)`
	    └─ `Chain.validate_block(block)`
			    └─ `Chain.validate_uncles(block)`
	*  `VM.pack_block(block)`
	    └─  `VMState.validate_block(block)`
			    └─`VMState.validate_uncle(block, uncle)`

    Regarding (5):
    * If `VMState` only handle “transaction level” state transition:
	    * The  `VMState.validate_block(block)` should be move to `VM`.
	    * With this change, we can also modify `VMState.prev_header` to simpler `VMState.prev_hashes`.
    * If `VMState` also handle “block level” state transition, should we move some more functions from `VM` to `VMState`?

issue_title: Add `loop` parameter to `wait_with_token` API
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

If I understand correctly:

[This call to `asyncio.wait`](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/fa07b6819c7f597bb5aa7ae13614d881a84081ae/p2p/cancel_token.py#L84) will used the *default* event loop if no loop parameter is provided

https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.wait

If that is the case, then in our tests, won't the cancel token be running in a different loop than other things that are using our local `event_loop` fixture?

### How can it be fixed

Change the `wait_with_token` API needs to accept an extra optional `loop` parameter.

issue_title: Transaction fail (Tx is Successful in Remix)
Error when reading multiple SimpleMRSs
issue_body:
This affects pyDelphin v0.4.1.

Parsing a single MRS works:

```python
>>> from delphin.mrs import simplemrs
>>> gen = simplemrs.loads('[ TOP: h0 RELS: < [ "_rain_v_1_rel" LBL: h1 ARG0: e2 ] > HCONS: < h0 qeq h1 > ]')
>>> next(gen)
<Xmrs object (rain) at 140066960825312>
```

But requesting the next object gives an `XmrsDeserializationError`, because the final `]` was not consumed:

```python
>>> next(gen)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/goodmami/repos/pydelphin/delphin/mrs/simplemrs.py", line 185, in deserialize
    yield _read_mrs(tokens, version, errors)
  File "/home/goodmami/repos/pydelphin/delphin/mrs/simplemrs.py", line 202, in _read_mrs
    _read_literals(tokens, '[')
  File "/home/goodmami/repos/pydelphin/delphin/mrs/simplemrs.py", line 195, in _read_literals
    'Expected \'{}\': {}'.format(tok, ' '.join([tok] + list(tokens)))
delphin._exceptions.XmrsDeserializationError: Expected '[': [
```

A `StopIteration` exception is expected for the second `next(gen)` call.

Also, the exception message is confusing, because it says the expected character is the one received, which isn't true.

issue_title: Don't break non-breaking spaces on SimpleMRS parsing
issue_body: During tokenization of SimpleMRS data, currently any space character (space, newline, tab, etc.) can terminate a token, outside of strings. But probably just space and newline is enough, since sometimes non-breaking spaces can be useful (see delph-in/erg#2).

issue_title: Add repodata information to extracted package directory
issue_body:
Immediately following the extraction of a package tarball, add the repodata information for the package to the `info` directory within the extracted package directory.  Put this information in a new file `info/external.json`.  Or maybe `info/post_extract.json`.  Or come up with a better name.

The information included should be all the information for the package under the `packages` key in repodata.  For example

    $ curl -sS https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/linux-64/repodata.json | jq .packages | jq '.["cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0.tar.bz2"]'

    {
      "build": "py27_0",
      "build_number": 0,
      "date": "2016-11-07",
      "depends": [
        "cffi >=1.4.1",
        "enum34",
        "idna >=2.0",
        "ipaddress",
        "openssl 1.0.2*",
        "pyasn1 >=0.1.8",
        "python 2.7*",
        "setuptools",
        "six >=1.4.1"
      ],
      "license": "Apache",
      "md5": "af03c1cde0584396f9422324701f6136",
      "name": "cryptography",
      "size": 829309,
      "version": "1.5.3"
    }

It should also include

* `fn` (which is the filename of the tarball)
* `url` (the exact, original url from where the tarball was downloaded)
* `arch` (which comes from the root-level `info` key in repodata)
* `platform` (which comes from the root-level `info` key in repodata)
* `priority` (the channel priority number at the time the package was downloaded)
* `channel` (which is right now equivalent to `Channel(full_url).base_url`
* `schannel` (equivalent to `Channel(full_url).canonical_name`)

I've probably missed some information, so feel free to add to the list.

The correct way to do this is probably to create a new PathAction in `conda/core/path_actions.py`, and then incorporate that action into the `ProgressiveFetchExtract.make_actions_for_dist` method in `conda/core/package_cache.py`.

issue_title: conda-build fails silently on Mac OS-X Darwin 64bit after upgrade to conda 4.x
issue_body:
After updating conda to 4.x one of my recipes fails silently on Mac OS-X Darwin 64bit.

The recipe:
```
package:
  name: my_package
  version: "0.1.9"

build:
  number: 0
  script: python setup.py install
  skip: True #[py26]

source:
  git_rev: v0.1.9
  git_url: [...]
requirements:
  build:
    - python
    - enum34 #[py27 or py33]
    - [..]

  run:
    - python
    - enum34 #[py27 or py33]
    - [..]

test:
  imports:
    - package
```
This is the (minimal) build command:
```
for python_version in 2.7 3.4 3.5
do
    conda-build --python ${python_version} MY_PACKAGE
done
```
And the relevant output:
```
BUILD START: my_package-0.1.9-py34_1

The package is not built, there is no indication of error. The recipe works flawlessly with the latest 3.x release.

issue_title: conda index on object storage
issue_body:
Hi! I'm currently investigating conda at Counsyl.  I've been looking through the docs on `conda index` and setting up an internal index for compiled binaries (we already have an internal pypi server).

Looking through the docs and implementation, seems like you could host a conda channel directly on object storage without too much work; the only issue is generating the repodata.json files.

Is there a way to tell conda index to crawl a repository over http but dump output files somewhere else?  Would that be an easy use case to support?  (then setting up a custom conda channel becomes as easy as syncing the output of build directories from running conda-bld, then dumping the results on to s3).

Failing that, is there a standard web server folks suggest to use for private channels? (It doesn't have to support authentication - VPN-only access is sufficient).

issue_title: Please use Semantic Versioning to signal backward incompatible changes.
issue_body:
The Conda version 4.4.0 (https://github.com/conda/conda/releases/tag/4.4.0) introduces backward-incompatible changes that breaks existing configurations.

To allow automatic Conda updates while avoid breaking changes, Conda should follow the Semantic Versioning convention:

Given a version number MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, increment the:

* MAJOR version when you make incompatible changes,
* MINOR version when you add functionality in a backwards-compatible manner, and
* PATCH version when you make backwards-compatible bug fixes.

For more information:
https://semver.org/

issue_title: pysam.libcbgzf.BGZFile object is not iterable
issue_body:
Im trying to read and write bgzfiles using pysam.libcbgzf.BGZFile but I get 

```TypeError: 'pysam.libcbgzf.BGZFile' object is not iterable```

Here is test code that can be run using pytest. Did I use it correctly or is this a bug?

using `pysam==0.10.0`

```
import pytest
import pysam
import gzip
import tempfile


@pytest.fixture(params=[True, False])
def input_as_row(request):
    return request.param


@pytest.fixture(params=["", "b"])
def as_binary(request):
    return request.param


@pytest.fixture(params=[
    "A\nB\nC\n",
    "\nAAA\t",
    "AAA",
    ""])
def input_data(request):
    return request.param


@pytest.fixture(params=[
    pysam.BGZFile,
    gzip.GzipFile,
    open])
def file_class(request):
    return request.param


def test_file(
        file_class,  # Different types of files
        input_data,  # Few different input strings
        input_as_row,  # Write rows or as one string
        as_binary):  # Open in binary mode
    output_data = ""
    with tempfile.TemporaryFile() as tmp:
        with file_class(tmp.name, "w{}".format(as_binary)) as f:
            if input_as_row:
                for row in input_data.splitlines(True):
                    f.write(row)
            else:
                for row in input_data:
                    f.write(row)

        with file_class(tmp.name, "r{}".format(as_binary)) as f:
            for row in f:
                output_data += row
    assert input_data == output_data
```

issue_title: Support for remote reference data missing in AlignmentFile
issue_body:
`AlignmentFile` takes an option `reference_filename`. Providing a remote reference FASTA file (e.g. pre-signed S3 URL) will result in `No such file or directory`. URLs work fine for the BAM/CRAM files.
In addition, please consider providing an option to specify the reference FASTA index file as an input since the index cannot be read in most use-cases using remote input data (similar to what is already available for FastxFile).

issue_title: paths/folders generated by vunit are not recognised as valid by modelsim
issue_body:

vunit_out/tests/lib.testbench.PARAMETER=X.test1/modelsim/vsim.wlf

issue_title: Adding colors to logging output
issue_body:
I've been testing colorized VUnit logging so that the output log shows different colors depending on the severity level. The goal is to make easier to spot interesting stuff on large logs.

![vunit_color](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6818668/16216626/a546f792-3742-11e6-9285-73b6cf549fbe.png)

issue_title: Shrink action leaves partially shrunk index on failure
issue_body:
I encountered the following situation using the shrink action. The action is set to delete the old index upon successful completion. Curator encounters an exception while executing the shrink action and leaves both indexes present on the cluster.

I believe this should not be normal behaviour as it prevents curator from working on the next run. The (unsuccessfully) shrunk index should be deleted upon exit if the action was unable to complete.

First run:
```
[...]
2017-09-14 16:33:24,471 INFO      Shrinking index "log-unknown-20170905" to "log-unknown-20170905-inactive" with settings: {'settings': {'index.number_of_shards': 1, 'index.number_of_replicas': 0, 'index.codec': 'best_compression'}}, wait_for_active_shards=1
2017-09-14 16:33:56,263 ERROR     Failed to complete action: shrink.  <class 'curator.exceptions.FailedExecution'>: Exception encountered.  Rerun with loglevel DEBUG and/or check Elasticsearch logs for more information. Exception: ConnectionTimeout caused by - ReadTimeoutError(HTTPConnectionPool(host='l4432s', port=9200): Read timed out. (read timeout=30))
```

Second run: 
```
2017-09-14 16:41:19,187 INFO      Preparing Action ID: 1, "shrink"
2017-09-14 16:41:19,283 INFO      Trying Action ID: 1, "shrink": Shrink index to one shard
2017-09-14 16:41:21,201 INFO      Source index: log-unknown-20170905 -- Target index: log-unknown-20170905-inactive
2017-09-14 16:41:21,372 ERROR     Failed to complete action: shrink.  <class 'curator.exceptions.FailedExecution'>: Exception encountered.  Rerun with loglevel DEBUG and/or check Elasticsearch logs for more information. Exception: Target index "log-unknown-20170905-inactive" already exists
```

On curator 5.2.0

Best,
Dan

issue_title: Delete marvel indices automatically
issue_body:
Hi, i installed curator to periodically delete the marvel indices. I've scheduled a windows task that runs every day, which executes a .bat file with the curator code. 
My code for Curator v.2 was this:  
curator delete --older-than 7 --prefix .marvel
Some days ago I upgraded curator to v.3, and i replaced my code with the following: 
curator delete indices --older-than 7 --prefix .marvel --time-unit days --timestring '%Y.%m.%d'

But it doesn't work. I read the documentation and seems that curator auto-prune the marvel indices to prevent accidental deletion.  
This was the reason why i installed curator: to be able to perform the delete-close-bloom-optimize  operations every day automatically, because the marvel data grows every day.

Is there a way to periodically delete the marvel data with curator?

**ES logs:**
```
[2018-03-05T04:03:44,735][INFO ][o.e.s.SnapshotShardsService] [0AB1Ezt] snapshot [repo:snapshot-20180305035936_<redacted>/2OKxaLkfSIm-Te5TWkamWQ] is done
[2018-03-05T04:03:44,897][WARN ][o.e.s.SnapshotsService   ] [0AB1Ezt] [repo:snapshot-20180305035936_<redacted>/2OKxaLkfSIm-Te5TWkamWQ] failed to finalize snapshot
org.elasticsearch.repositories.RepositoryException: [repo] concurrent modification of the index-N file, expected current generation [1290], actual current generation [1293] - possibly due to simultaneous snapshot deletion requests
        at org.elasticsearch.repositories.blobstore.BlobStoreRepository.writeIndexGen(BlobStoreRepository.java:825) ~[elasticsearch-5.5.3.jar:5.5.3]
        at org.elasticsearch.repositories.blobstore.BlobStoreRepository.finalizeSnapshot(BlobStoreRepository.java:564) ~[elasticsearch-5.5.3.jar:5.5.3]
        at org.elasticsearch.snapshots.SnapshotsService$5.run(SnapshotsService.java:950) [elasticsearch-5.5.3.jar:5.5.3]
        at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.ThreadContext$ContextPreservingRunnable.run(ThreadContext.java:569) [elasticsearch-5.5.3.jar:5.5.3]
        at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1142) [?:1.8.0_131]
        at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:617) [?:1.8.0_131]
        at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:748) [?:1.8.0_131]
```

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
Snapshotting ~20 individual indices (1 per action) using the same structure as the snippet shown below. Index sizes range from 200kb - 1.5tb, however the index that failed above had a size of ~15gb.

**Action File Snippet:**
```
SNAPSHOT_DAILY_<redacted>:
  action: snapshot
  description: "daily index: <redacted>"
  options:
    repository: 'repo'
    name: 'snapshot-%Y%m%d%H%M%S_<redacted>'
    ignore_empty_list: True
    continue_if_exception: True
    disable_action: False
  filters:
  - filtertype: kibana
    exclude: True
  - filtertype: period
    source: name
    range_from: -1
    range_to: -1
    timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
    unit: days
  - filtertype: pattern
    kind: prefix
    value: <redacted>
```

### Specifications
  - Version: Curator 5.1.1; ES 5.5.3
  - Platform: Python 2.7.5; Rhel7

## Context (Environment)
This issue started showing up once I updated the snapshotting strategy based on structural changes. Went from single cluster single snapshot for all Daily indices (which had high likelihood of causing partial snapshots) to multi-cluster where each cluster had action file with 1 action per daily index (giving more granularity and visibility on snapshots that fail).

We install the azure cli on an Ubuntu 14.04 VM using apt-get in some of our automation scenarios and we started getting a new error yesterday. After running `az login`, we see this:
> Please ensure you have network connection. Error detail: Can't connect to HTTPS URL because the SSL module is not available.

 For reference this is how we install the cli:
```
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y libssl-dev libffi-dev python-dev
echo "deb [arch=amd64] https://apt-mo.trafficmanager.net/repos/azure-cli/ wheezy main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/azure-cli.list
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver apt-mo.trafficmanager.net --recv-keys 417A0893
sudo apt-get install -y apt-transport-https
sudo apt-get -y update && sudo apt-get install -y azure-cli
```

Like I said, we do this in automation (aka it's a clean VM every time) and we haven't changed anything about how we install the CLI. I tried interactively installing with the `https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCli` command and did _not_ reproduce this error.

issue_title: Table output supresses IDs and Types but in some cases, it would be beneficial
issue_body:
Table output hides IDs and types by default as ids aren't table-friendly and types are typically not needed. e.g. az vm list -otable would show Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines in each row of the table.
However, there are special cases (e.g. Storage data-plane commands and `az resource show -otable`) where showing IDs/types in table output would be useful.

A workaround is to create a custom callable table format for those specific commands that need to display IDs or Types.

Related to https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1003 and https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1039.

issue_title: conditions JSON format required is not syntactically correct JSON
issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**

The only JSON which the Azure cloud will accept for creating a Monitoring rule is as below:

{
    "dataSource": {
      "odata.type": "Microsoft.Azure.Management.Insights.Models.RuleMetricDataSource",
      "resourceUri": "/subscriptions/your_subscription/resourceGroups/your_resource_group/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/hcm99mon01",
      "metricName": "Percentage CPU",
    },
    "odata.type": "Microsoft.Azure.Management.Insights.Models.ThresholdRuleCondition",
    "threshold" : 60,
    "operator": "GreaterThan",
    "windowSize" : "PT5M",
    "timeAggregation": "Average"
}

The comma after  "metricName": "Percentage CPU",

is not correct JSON as there are no items following the comma.
Yet the Azure cloud will say "poorly formed request" when the comma is removed.

The JSON code above will work in Azure cloud to create a metric based rule.

But will fail JSON validation at the JSON validation LINT site:

https://jsonlint.com/


---

**Install Method:** How did you install the CLI? (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, nightly)

Nightly then installed public / bash

Answer here:

3.0.2

Monitor 0.0.3

**OS Version:** What OS and version are you using?  
Answer here:

macOS Sierra

**Shell Type:** What shell are you using? (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  
Answer here:

csh

**Description**:
Our VSTS build definition contains an Azure CLI task (to upload templates to Azure). The whole task is marked as failed because **az storage blob upload-batch** command writes info messages into **stderr** stream. 
For example: "WARNING: uploading C:\xxxxxxx\AzureDeployment\Templates\demo.parameters.json"

The behavior can be simulated locally as well. Let's create a simple console app which starts a process and redirects the standard error stream of application.

issue_title: Websocket flow control is broken (deadlock+memory leak on websocket.receive)
issue_body:
## Long story short

Hello! I've ran into serious problem while implementing custom RPC over websockets (aiohttp on both sides). On a dense stream of larger messages consumer just hangs (while eating gigabytes of memory). Sender is "fine" (await ws.send_bytes() proceeds).

It seems to be correlated with message size, but I'm not sure. Surfaces randomly, but quite often on larger (64k+) messages. Who is client and who is server isn't important, so I'll stick to sender/consumer terms.

The problem appeared only on actual distributed case, if sender-consumer are both on localhost everything works fine.

Why do I think that issue is somewhere in flow control - the usecase worked fine on older versions of aiohttp before it was implemented.

Any feedback is much appreciated )

## Expected behaviour

* Consumer doesn't hang
* Sender blocks if needed

## Actual behaviour

* On consumer's side: `await ws.receive` (or `async for msg in ws`) hangs at some point, memory grows to infinity and beyond
* On sender's side: everything looks fine (but you'll never get any response as consumer is dead)
* Even more interesting: *very rarely* consumer shows an error and disconnects

## Steps to reproduce

Original program was quite convoluted, so I've made a simple example reproducing the issue.

Consumer script (implemented as server):

```
import aiohttp.web

async def consumer(request):
    ws = aiohttp.web.WebSocketResponse()
    await ws.prepare(request)

    msg_index = 0
    async for msg in ws:
        msg_index += 1
        print("Block", msg.type, msg_index)
    return ws


def main():
    app = aiohttp.web.Application()
    app.router.add_get("/ws", consumer)
    aiohttp.web.run_app(app, port=9090)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

Sender script (implemented as client):

```
import asyncio
import aiohttp
import uuid

async def main():
    # can use larger number for more chance to hang, but beware of consumer's memory use
    # with provided constant values it'll peak at ~1.3Gb
    BLOCK_COUNT = 5000
    BLOCK_SIZE_MULTIPLIER = 2**14
    # uuid4 is 16 bytes, 256k block total size
    BLOCK_SIZE = 16 * BLOCK_SIZE_MULTIPLIER
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        # TODO: use your own IP :)
        async with session.ws_connect("http://xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:9090/ws") as ws:
            for block_idx in range(BLOCK_COUNT):
                await ws.send_bytes(uuid.uuid4().bytes * BLOCK_SIZE_MULTIPLIER)
                print("Block", block_idx + 1, "sent")
    
if __name__ == '__main__':
  asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(main())
```

Sender prints everything up to "Block 5000 sent". Consumer **often, but not always** stops somewhere much eariler (seen 293, 3097 - completely random).


## Your environment

* 2 machines, Ubuntu 16.04
* Python 3.6
* aiohttp 2.0.7
* Gbit LAN

issue_title: RuntimeError raised when a Redirect doesn't have a Location or URI HTTP header
issue_body:
## Long story short

As per title, whenever a redirect response doesn't have a Location or a URI HTTP header, a RuntimeError will be raised by aiohttp. While this is indeed a problem, I think that raising RuntimeError seems a bit too much, since now I have to handle it explicitly in my code, rather than catching an aiohttp exception

## Expected behaviour

For another exception, particular to aiohttp, to be raised, so that we can handle this case from our code, without hiding other bugs.

## Actual behaviour

RuntimeError gets raised

## Steps to reproduce

```
import aiohttp
import asyncio

async def fetch(session, url):
        async with session.get(url) as response:
            return await response.text()

async def main():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        html = await fetch(session, 'http://www.droidreport.com/googles-market-android-wearables-7173')
        print(html)

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.run_until_complete(main())
```

## Your environment

aiohttp==2.0.7
Python 3.6

issue_title: Exception on _pong_not_received for websockets
issue_body:
## Long story short
Hi!
An exception occurs when closing the websocket connection in _pong_not_received if the connection was already dropped somehow :
```
  File "aiohttp/web_ws.py", line 88, in _pong_not_received
    self._req.transport.close()
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'close'
```

## Expected behaviour

No exception.

## Actual behaviour

`self._req` is not `None`, but `self._req.transport` is `None`.

## Your environment

Python 3.5, aiohttp==2.0.5, standard loop, server behind nginx.

The issue is happening with really bad websocket clients, not web browsers, that may drop connections without sending `close` control frames.

Thanks !

issue_title: RequestHandler._task_handler cancellation is never awaited
issue_body:
in `connection_lost`, if there is an active `self._task_handler`, it is cancelled, however it is then never awaited resulting in the warning: `Task was destroyed but it is pending`

I did a simple change to:
```
        if self._task_handler and not self._task_handler.done():
            self._task_handler.cancel()
            asyncio.ensure_future(self._task_handler, loop=self_loop)
```

and the warning went away, not sure if this is the right thing to do.

There's another problem though, if you have an expired certificate, the `task_handler` will be thrown a `GeneratorExit` with a different task and can cause all kinds of weird problems in client code that has context handlers after `start()`.  Would be nice to pinpoint where its coming from so it can be handled lower-level

issue_body:
<!--

### `nbgrader --version`
0.5.4
### `jupyterhub --version` (if used with JupyterHub)
0.8.1
### `jupyter notebook --version` 
5.2.2


We are two instructors for a course and nbgrader home is shared directory between us. Both instructors are part of same linux group and we manage file permissions under nbgrader home at this group level. Everything works fine for us, we are both able to create/release/autograde assignments. However, manual grading is creating problems for one of us. When one of us try to manually grade an assignment, we get a 404 error while the same thing works for the other.

issue_title: can't disable autoclose brackets - I swear those extra closing brackets are stalking me!
Build command broken on Windows
issue_body:
Running `setup.py build` on Windows breaks on the CSS command. This is due to excessive quoting of the command by the `run` function.

[This change](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/commit/f175b2391ae468de39831e65674012c9543b0ccb) introduced an extra level of quoting to all commands. 

On Windows, the `lessc` command list looks like the following:

```
['lessc', '--source-map', "--include-path='E:\\Development\\sccolbert\\notebook\\notebook\\static'", 'E:\\Development\\sccolbert\\notebook\\notebook\\static\\style\\ipython.less', 'E:\\Development\\sccolbert\\notebook\\notebook\\static\\style\\ipython.min.css']
```

Which would be valid if joined with spaces. But with the extra quoting, the command is invalid on Windows:

```
lessc --source-map '--include-path='"'"'E:\Development\sccolbert\notebook\notebook\static'"'"'' 'E:\Development\sccolbert\notebook\notebook\static\style\ipython.less' 'E:\Development\sccolbert\notebook\notebook\static\style\ipython.min.css'
```

The [docs](https://docs.python.org/2/library/pipes.html#pipes.quote) for `pipes.quote` indicate that the quoting is only compatible with Unix shells.

On my machine, doing a simple space-join is sufficient to fix the breakage. But, I'm not sure if this will cause breakage on other platforms.

pinging @jdfreder @minrk

issue_title: add support for SHA2 in the curl plugin
RFC: Add "publish_github_prerelease" add-ons
issue_body:
### Overview

This add-on allows to automatically publish pre-release on GitHub. 

A pre-release is identified by a git tag and is associated with one or multiple packages (python wheels, installers, archives, ...)

Project maintainers are responsible to graduate a "pre-release" into "release".


###  Use cases

#### Automatic upload of python wheels associated with  [ITKPythonPackage](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage) project

##### Steps

Every day a *bot* will:
* update ITK description and package version in  `ITKPythonPackage` (see details below)
* commit the changes to `ITKPythonPackage`. 

Then, each CI services (or dedicated build bots) will:
* checkout `ITKPythonPackage` source
* build the associated wheels
* use `publish_github_prerelease.py` to publish the generated wheels as pre-release

##### `update_python_version.py` script

This script is not (yet) part of `scikit-ci-addons` and will live in the `ITKPythonPackage` project repository and can be executed as a standalone script. For example, it could then be downloaded and executed by the *bot* named  [kwrobot](https://github.com/kwrobot).

**Usage**

```
Usage:
  python update_python_version.py /path/to/last/ITK/source
```

**Steps**

The *bot* will:
* clone the latest `ITK` source, checkout `nightly-master` branch
* clone `ITKPythonPackage`
* download the script
* execute the script
* commit and push the change to `ITKPythonPackage`

**Script description: update_python_version.py**

Given the patch to both an `ITK` source tree and an `ITKPythonPackage` source tree, the script will:
* get the local commit date of the `HEAD` of [ITK@nightly-master](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITK/tree/nightly-master). (Using a command like `git show -s --format="%cd" --date=local`)
* get `<major>.<minor>.<patch>` (By extracting the value associated with variables `ITK_VERSION_MAJOR`, `ITK_VERSION_MINOR` and `ITK_VERSION_PATCH` using script like [CMakeParser.py](https://github.com/Slicer/Slicer/blob/eefeac9286f48552e8418a11f412b2823a09b407/Utilities/Scripts/SlicerWizard/CMakeParser.py))
* update [CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt) replacing the ITK version associated with `GIT_TAG` CMake variable to match the latest commit associated with [ITK@nightly-master](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITK/tree/nightly-master)
* update [setup.py](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage/blob/master/setup.py) by replacing version<sup>1</sup> `<major>.<minor>.<patch>.dev<YYYYMMDD>` where `<major>.<minor>.<patch>` corresponds the ITK release version and `<YYYYMMDD>` corresponds to the date of the `ITK@nightly-master` commit

<sup>1</sup> To facilitate the update of the version, it could be added to a separate file like `itkVersion.py` or `itk/version.py`

**example of execution**

On unix:

```
# TODO: Delete old virtual env and create a new one (e.g name itk-nightly-wheel)

pip install scikit-ci-addons
git clone git://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage
cd ITKPythonPackage

platform=$(ci_addons publish_github_release --current-platform) # manylinux1, macosx or win
commit_date=$(ci_addons git --commit_date)

./scripts/dockcross-manylinux-build-wheels.sh && \
ci_addons publish_github_release  \
  --clear-pattern "*$platform*.whl" \
  --keep-pattern "*dev$commit_date*.whl" \
  InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage \
  "dist/*dev$commit_date*$platform*.whl"
```

**Remarks:** Invocation of `ci_addons publish_github_release` should probably be moved into  `./scripts/dockcross-manylinux-build-wheels.sh` and `./scripts/macosx-build-wheels.sh`

On windows:

```
$PYTHON_DIR="C:/Python27-x64"
$env:PATH="$PYTHON_DIR;$PYTHON_DIR/Scripts;$env:PATH"

# TODO: Delete old virtual env and create a new one (e.g name itk-nightly-wheel)

pip install scikit-ci-addons
pip install ninja
git clone git://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage
cd ITKPythonPackage

$platform=(ci_addons publish_github_release --current-platform) # manylinux1, macosx or win
$commit_date=(ci_addons git --commit_date)

# Adapted from http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2251622/conditional-execution-and-in-powershell#7272878
$(python setup.py bdist_wheel | Out-Host;$?) -and `
$(
  ci_addons publish_github_release  `
    --clear-pattern "*$platform*.whl" `
    --keep-pattern "*dev$commit_date*.whl" `
    InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKPythonPackage `
    "dist/*dev$commit_date*$platform*.whl"
| Out-Host;$?)
```

**Remarks:** Invocation of `ci_addons publish_github_release` should probably be moved into  `./scripts/windows-build-wheels.ps1`

issue_title: Explore ideas for DHT incentives / storage policies
issue_body:
Currently, there are two types of incentives for running DHT nodes:

a) Someone running blockstored, is automatically a full DHT node as well (but I can imagine lightweight implementations where people try to turn that functionality off)

b) Companies (such as [Onename](http://onename.com)) providing public DHT servers

It'll be great to brainstorm better incentives for running DHT nodes. Also, to combat SPAM there could be mechanisms where the "storage policy" at DHT nodes requires proof of "something", where that something could be a blockstore transaction.

If we'd like others to create namespaces, we should make the price easily discoverable.

Do we have one? If not, we should add one.

issue_title: Explain how to set up API as part of full node instructions
issue_body:
I tried to set up a full node over the past couple days. 

The docker instructions didn't work for me at all (#656). The pip install instructions got the indexer running for me once I re-imaged my virtual machine with a clean install of Ubuntu 16.04. (instructions didn't tell me to do this)  However, they didn't explain to me how get the Core API running in a way that points to my node.

After installing core and letting it index, I ended up with an API endpoint pointing to node.blockstack.org. I tried changing the node.blockstack.org entry in `client.ini` to localhost, but that didn't work. Not sure if it's because another setting (https?) needs to be changed.

Instructions for setting up a full core node that don't tell you how to enable the REST api so you can actually use the full core node you've just set up aren't very useful.

As we've previously discussed, the main installation path for developers and users is downloading and installing Blockstack for their respective platform from our website. Having said that, as a decentralized platform, it's important that people can run their own nodes.  

The current documentation doesn't provide step by step instructions that work without additional guessing or troubleshooting on the part of someone following the instructions.

issue_title: default CLI args not over-written by config settings
issue_body:
I have a config file that contains

``` [server]
port=7000
```

However, the server still uses the default port value (5696).  I see that is because
1. the OptionParser has the "port" option using a default of 5696. This causes KmipServer._setup_configuration() to have its 'port' parameter set to 5696
2. KmipServer._setup_configuration() calls self.config.load_settings() _before_ handling the settings passed in its input arguments.

Fixing this may be as simple as moving the call to config.load_settings() down a few lines (after the handling of the input parameters).

issue_title: Suppress protected-access warning about User._default_manager()
issue_body:
Hi

For this method ([here](https://github.com/mozilla/treeherder/blob/0ea4717230c2172e9dbde2ca7897cd5ab276f919/treeherder/auth/backends.py#L151-L156)):

```python
from django.contrib.auth.models import User


class AuthBackend(object):

    # ...

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        try:
            return User._default_manager.get(pk=user_id)
        except User.DoesNotExist:
            return None
```

pylint warns about:
```
Access to a protected member _default_manager of a client class (W0212: protected-access)
```

This seems like something that pylint-django might be able to suppress? (Given that `._default_manager()` is an [official API](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/db/managers/#django.db.models.Model._default_manager))

The above was using:
Python 2.7.14
pylint==1.8.4
pylint-django==0.9.4
pylint-plugin-utils==0.2.6
astroid==1.6.3

issue_title: Improve coverage setup in repo
issue_body:
There are a couple of odd things with the coverage configuration here:

- The `tests/` directory is included under coverage sources, so coverage reports coverage on the tests files. This is quite odd; coverage on the test modules themselves is seldom interesting, and in general people only want to see coverage on the source.
- The coverage status doesn't feed into any integration-style reports. I'm not sure what the requirements around coverage are for PRs, but given that all the work in setting up coverage is done, it's pretty easy to enable e.g. Codecov to report coverage changes in PRs.

Also, at this point it'd probably be a bit cleaner to use pytest-cov than to invoke coverage directly.

I'd be happy to open a PR to address these if these are legitimate things worth addressing.

issue_title: Research Host.puppet_ca_proxy
issue_body:
The semantics of the `Host.puppet_ca_proxy` field are not well understood. As a result, the field is omitted. The API documentation mentions it, though. From the API documentation:

> POST /api/hosts
> host[puppet_ca_proxy_id]
> Value: Must be a number.

Research the semantics of this field and add it to `nailgun.entities` if appropriate.

issue_title: Merge dict variables
issue_body:
This is a new proposed feature for ``pytest-variables``: merge dict variables instead of replace a key value based on command line order.

For example for a given command line option ``--variables capabilities/WIN10-FIREFOX53.json --variables capabilities capabilities/debug.json``

where the content of the ``capabilities/WIN10-FIREFOX53.json`` file is:
```
{                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
    "MYPROJECT": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
        "capabilities": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
            "browser": "Firefox",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
            "browser_version": "53.0",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
            "os": "Windows",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
            "os_version": "10",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
            "resolution": "1280x1024"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
        }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
}
```

and the content of the ``capabilities/debug.json`` file is:
```                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
{                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
    "MYPROJECT": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
        "capabilities": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
            "browserstack.debug": true                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
        }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    }
}
```

the result should be a merged dict like that:
```
{                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
    "MYPROJECT": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
        "capabilities": {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
            "browser": "Firefox",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
            "browser_version": "53.0",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
            "os": "Windows",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
            "os_version": "10",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
            "resolution": "1280x1024",
            "browserstack.debug": true                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
        }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
}
```

Does it make sense? If yes I'm available for voluntary work :)

issue_title: Create package for Raspbian OS (ARM32)
issue_body:
_From @ygoe on June 30, 2018 14:13_

While there is a working binary download for dotnet (.NET Core) for ARM32 that works on a Raspberry Pi B 3+ with a current Raspbian OS, there are no instructions how to install it. And there are no packages for the OS, too. So it's a bit of a mystery how to actually make .NET Core work on a Raspi.

I think there should be a package for that OS. It should be as simple as that:

    sudo apt install dotnet

Maybe a custom package source needs to be added, but definitely no manual tar file works. Mono was easier to install when it was current like 5 years ago.

Since the docker support on the Raspi with dotnet-runtime packages is very poor (multi-arch fails, need to select arm32 image specifically, can't get my app in a custom image), I need to install .NET Core on the Raspi OS itself.

issue_title: Useable for intranet / behind FW servers?
issue_body:
I have a question concerning useability for intranet based / behind the firewall servers.

We like to change everything to HTTPS, including our intranet servers, and (at least personally) I'd like to do that with "normal" certs, i.e. not self-signed. Less trouble with pushing our self-signing root cert to clients, trust store management etc. etc. etc.

I thought letsencrypt would be the ideal alternative, but from reading the specs etc I'm getting the impression that using letsencrypt behind the firewall might be difficult if not impossible.

Is it possible? Perhaps in manual mode?

issue_title: renew --dry-run shouldn't treat rate limits as outright failures
issue_body:
Since (1) being staging-ratelimited isn't the same thing as being prod-ratelimited and (2) for most people it'll be fine in the end.

Instead, let's print a note ("Rate limited, but by the test server not the production server") and count these as successes.  And then maybe link to public docs on rate limit in case anyone is anxious or needs to know more.

issue_title: Error using cc or bcc with recipients substitution_data
issue_body:
At some point in the past couple of weeks, SparkPost started rejecting the cc recipient in the following call:

``` python
from sparkpost import SparkPost
sp = SparkPost()
result = sp.transmissions.send(
    from_email='test@example.com',  # use a valid sender for your account
    recipients=[{
        'address': {'email': 'to@example.com'},
        'substitution_data': {'order_no': '12345'}
    }],
    cc=['cc@example.com'],
    subject='test',
    text='test merge {{ order_no }}'
)
# {'total_rejected_recipients': 1, 'total_accepted_recipients': 1, 'id': '102374996990914834', 'rcpt_to_errors': [{'message': 'invalid data format/type', 'code': '1300', 'description': 'field \'recipients[1].address.header_to\' value \'{ "substitution_data": { "order_no": "12345" }, "address": { "email": "to@example.com" } }\' is of type \'json_object\', but needs to be of type \'string\''}]}
```

The problem seems to be in the payload's `header_to` generated for the `cc`. If you omit the `cc` parameter, it works fine. Similarly, if you change to a simple `recipients=['to@example.com']`, the send is accepted (albeit without the intended substitution data).

This worked (or at least was accepted by the API) on 2016-06-07. I don't believe python-sparkpost has changed during that period. Is this a problem in python-sparkpost, or an intentional API clarification/change? If the latter, is there way to specify substitution_data and cc (or bcc) in the same transmission?

python 3.5 (or 2.7); python-sparkpost 1.2.0

issue_title: Make relativedelta more inheritance friendly
issue_body:
Migrating this issue from launchpad issue [#1010199](https://bugs.launchpad.net/dateutil/+bug/1010199).

Quoting [Shai Berger](https://launchpad.net/~shai-platonix):
>Hi,

>I needed to subclass relativedelta. Then, it was disappointing to find that the __add__, __sub__ and __neg__ operations explicitly return a new relativedelta object; it would be much nicer if they could return a self.__class__ object.

>Thanks,
>Shai.

issue_title: Python3.4 compatibility broken (again)
issue_body:
Another issue with Python3.4: 

```
  File "/home/nemo/github/Telethon/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 1080, in _recv_thread_impl
    self.updates.set_error(e)
  File "/home/nemo/github/Telethon/telethon/update_state.py", line 60, in set_error
    self._updates.insert(0, error)
AttributeError: 'collections.deque' object has no attribute 'insert'

```
Because [collections.deque.insert](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.html#collections.deque.insert) was introduced with Python 3.5.

(I guess you don't run/test Telethon on Python3.4. Maybe we can keep this issue open, in case I find more problems ...)

issue_title: Better caching for uploaded files
issue_body:
See https://t.me/TelethonChat/36584.

The basic idea is that attributes should also be cached to:
1. Reupload the file with the new attributes automatically instead of silently using the old ones.
2. Avoid always `allow_cache=False` foreseeing the first point.

Sorting by constructor ID and saving its bytes (or their hash?) should be enough.

issue_title: Connection Reset on Heroku
issue_body:
I'm trying to deploy an app to Herkou, but the tcp client got Connection Reset, so i'm unable to connect to telegram,

i'm using the version `telethon = "==0.15.5.7"`,

here the log
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "app_main.py", line 15, in main
    kernel = Kernel(config, users)
  File "/app/utils/Singleton.py", line 7, in get_instance
    instances[cls] = cls(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/app/app/Kernel.py", line 71, in __init__
    self.clients.append(self.create_client(user))
  File "/app/app/Kernel.py", line 127, in create_client
    client.inject(i)
  File "/app/app/clients/ChatClient.py", line 27, in inject
    if issubclass(data,ChatModule) : self.load_module(data)
  File "/app/app/clients/ChatClient.py", line 50, in load_module
    module.load(self)
  File "/app/app/contracts/ChatModule.py", line 94, in load
    self.id = self.client.client.get_entity(self.recipient).id
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 1052, in get_entity
    return self._get_entity_from_string(entity)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_client.py", line 1078, in _get_entity_from_string
    self(ResolveUsernameRequest(entity))
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_bare_client.py", line 439, in __call__
    sender, call_receive, update_state, *requests
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_bare_client.py", line 479, in _invoke
    authenticator.do_authentication(self._sender.connection)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/network/authenticator.py", line 27, in do_authentication
    return _do_authentication(connection)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/network/authenticator.py", line 45, in _do_authentication
    sender.send(bytes(req_pq_request))
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/network/mtproto_plain_sender.py", line 30, in send
    struct.pack('<QQi', 0, self._get_new_msg_id(), len(data)) + data
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/network/connection.py", line 182, in _send_tcp_full
    self.write(data + crc)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/network/connection.py", line 219, in _write_plain
    self.conn.write(data)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/extensions/tcp_client.py", line 108, in write
    raise ConnectionResetError()
ConnectionResetError
```

as you can see the error is on  `client.get_entity` method, but i cannot understand why.

-Thanks

issue_title: Cannot make job: :1:1: Expression evaluation error:
issue_body:
---

## Expected Behavior
This was a working WF at version 1.0.20170622090721, should execute to completion.  I cleaned out  `~/.local` then `pip install --user cwltool` 

## Actual Behavior
Fails on first step, see below.

## Workflow Code
```
#!/usr/bin/env cwl-runner

class: Workflow
cwlVersion: v1.0

requirements:
- $import: tools/trimmomatic-types.yml
- $import: tools/envvar-global.yml
- class: InlineJavascriptRequirement
  expressionLib:
  - var rename_trim_file = function() {
      if ( self == null ) {
        return null;
      } else {
        var xx = self.basename.split('.');
        var id = xx.indexOf('fastq');
        xx.splice(id, 1);
        return xx.join('.');
      }
    };
- class: ScatterFeatureRequirement
- class: StepInputExpressionRequirement

inputs:
  read1: File
  read2: File

outputs:
  # trim with trimmomatic and rename
  trim-logs:
    type: File
    outputSource: trim/output_log
  rename_reads1_trimmed_file:
    type: File
    outputSource: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
  rename_reads2_trimmed_paired_file:
    type: File
    outputSource: trim/reads2_trimmed_unpaired
  reads1_trimmed_unpaired_file:
    type: File
    outputSource: trim/reads1_trimmed_unpaired
  reads2_trimmed_unpaired_file:
    type: File
    outputSource: trim/reads2_trimmed_unpaired
  # align to mouse with bowtie2
  mouse-aligned:
    type: File
    outputSource: align-to-mouse/aligned-file
  # align to human with bowtie2
  human-aligned:
    type: File
    outputSource: align-to-human/aligned-file
  # convert
  human-sorted:
    type: File
    streamable: true
    outputSource: convert-human/output
  # sort and compress
  human-compress:
    type: File
    outputSource: sort-human/sorted
  # Index human bam
  human-index:
    type: File
    outputSource: index-human/index
  # compare genomes with xenomapper
  primary_specific:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/primary_specific
  secondary_specific:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/secondary_specific
  primary_multi:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/primary_multi
  secondary_multi:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/secondary_multi
  unassigned:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/unassigned
  unresolved:
    type: File?
    outputSource: xenomapping/unresolved
  # pileip
  human-mpileup:
    type: File
    outputSource: mpileup-human/output
  # varscan output
  varscan:
    type: File
    outputSource: varscan-human/output
  # platypus output
  platypus-vcf:
    type: File
    outputSource: platypus/output
  # gridss
  gridss:
    type: File
    outputSource: gridss-human/output

steps:

  #
  # trim with trimmomatic
  #
  trim:
    run: tools/trimmomatic.cwl

    in:
      reads1:
        source: read1
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              self.format = "http://edamontology.org/format_1930";
              return self;
          }
      reads2:
        source: read2
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              self.format = "http://edamontology.org/format_1930";
              return self;
          }
      end_mode:
        default: PE
      nthreads:
        valueFrom: ${ return 8; }
      illuminaClip:
        default:
          adapters:
            class: File
            location: "/stornext/System/data/apps/trimmomatic/trimmomatic-0.36/adapters/TruSeq3-PE.fa"
          seedMismatches: 1
          palindromeClipThreshold: 20
          simpleClipThreshold: 20
          minAdapterLength: 4
          keepBothReads: "true"

    out: [output_log, reads1_trimmed, reads1_trimmed_unpaired, reads2_trimmed_paired, reads2_trimmed_unpaired]

  #
  # rename trimmed files by removing redundant '.fastq' from the filename
  #
  rename_reads1_trimmed:
    run: tools/rename-file.cwl

    in:
      infile: trim/reads1_trimmed
      outfile:
        source: trim/reads1_trimmed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return rename_trim_file();
          }

    out: [renamed]

  rename_reads2_trimmed_paired:
    run: tools/rename-file.cwl

    in:
      infile: trim/reads2_trimmed_paired
      outfile:
        source: trim/reads2_trimmed_paired
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return rename_trim_file();
          }

    out: [renamed]

  #
  # align to mouse reference with bowtie2
  #
  align-to-mouse:
    run: tools/bowtie2.cwl

    in:
      samout:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.mouse.sam'
          }
      threads:
        valueFrom: ${ return 8; }
      one:
        source: trim/reads1_trimmed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return [self];
          }
      two:
        source: rename_reads2_trimmed_paired/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            if ( self == null ) {
              return null;
              } else {
              return [self];
            }
          }
      unpaired:
        source: trim/reads1_trimmed_unpaired
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            if ( self == null ) {
              return null;
              } else {
              return [self];
            }
          }
      bt2-idx:
        default: /stornext/HPCScratch/PapenfussLab/reference_genomes/bowtie2/GRCm38
      local:
        default: true
      reorder:
        default: true

    out: [aligned-file]

  #
  # align to human reference with bowtie2
  #
  align-to-human:
    run: tools/bowtie2.cwl

    in:
      samout:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.human.sam'
          }
      threads:
        valueFrom: ${ return 8; }
      one:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return [self];
          }
      two:
        source: rename_reads2_trimmed_paired/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            if ( self == null ) {
              return null;
              } else {
              return [self];
            }
          }
      unpaired:
        source: trim/reads1_trimmed_unpaired
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            if ( self == null ) {
              return null;
              } else {
              return [self];
            }
          }
      bt2-idx:
        default: /wehisan/bioinf/bioinf-data/Papenfuss_lab/projects/reference_genomes/human_new/no_alt/hg38_no_alt.fa
      local:
        default: true
      reorder:
        default: true

    out: [aligned-file]

  #
  # convert human
  #
  convert-human:
    run: tools/samtools-view.cwl

    in:
      input:
        align-to-human/aligned-file
      output_name:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.human.bam'
          }
      threads:
        valueFrom: ${ return 8; }

    out: [output]

  #
  # sort and compress human
  #
  sort-human:
    run: tools/samtools-sort.cwl

    in:
      input:
        source: convert-human/output
      output_name:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.sorted.human.bam'
          }
      threads:
        valueFrom: ${ return 8; }

    out: [sorted]

  #
  # index human bam
  #
  index-human:
    run: tools/samtools-index.cwl

    in:
      input:
        source: sort-human/sorted

    out: [index]

  #
  # human pileup file
  #
  mpileup-human:
    run: tools/samtools-mpileup.cwl

    in:
      bamFiles:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              if ( self == null ) {
                return null;
              } else {
                return [self];
              }
            }

      output_fn:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.pileup'
          }

    out: [output]

  #
  # varscan human
  #
  varscan-human:
    run: tools/varscan-mpileup2snp.cwl

    in:
      input:
        source: mpileup-human/output

      output-vcf:
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return true;
          }

    out: [output]

  #
  # GRIDSS that human
  #
  gridss-human:
    run: tools/gridss-callvariants.cwl

    in:
      INPUT:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              if ( self == null ) {
                return null;
              } else {
                return [self];
              }
            }

      REFERENCE_SEQUENCE:
        default: /wehisan/bioinf/bioinf-data/Papenfuss_lab/projects/reference_genomes/human_new/no_alt/hg38_no_alt.fa

      OUTPUT:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.gridss.vcf'
          }

      ASSEMBLY:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.gridss.bam'
          }

    out: [output]

  #
  # xenomapper
  #
  xenomapping:
    run: tools/xenomapper.cwl

    in:
      primary_sam:
        source: align-to-human/aligned-file
      secondary_sam:
        source: align-to-mouse/aligned-file
      primary_specific_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.human_specific.sam'
          }
      secondary_specific_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.mouse_specific.sam'
          }
      primary_multi_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.human_multi.sam'
          }
      secondary_multi_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.mouse_multi.sam'
          }
      unassigned_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.unassigned.sam'
          }
      unresolved_fn:
        source: rename_reads1_trimmed/renamed
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return self.nameroot + '.unresolved.sam'
          }

    out: [primary_specific, secondary_specific, primary_multi, secondary_multi, unassigned, unresolved]

  #
  # Gather human bam and index into one dependency
  #
  gather:
    run:
      class: ExpressionTool

      inputs:
        bamFile:
          type: File
        bamIndex:
          type: File

      outputs:
        combined:
          type: File

      expression: >
        ${
          var ret = inputs.bamFile;
          ret["secondaryFiles"] = [
            inputs.bamIndex
          ];
          return {"combined" : ret};
        }

    in:
      bamFile:
        source: sort-human/sorted
      bamIndex:
        source: index-human/index

    out: [combined]

  #
  # Call with platypus
  #
  platypus:
    run: tools/platypus.cwl

    in:
      bamFiles:
        source: gather/combined
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            if ( self == null ) {
              return null;
              } else {
              return [self];
            }
          }
      refFile:
        default: /wehisan/bioinf/bioinf-data/Papenfuss_lab/projects/reference_genomes/human_new/no_alt/hg38_no_alt.fa
      outputFileName:
        source: sort-human/sorted
        valueFrom: >
          ${
              return self.nameroot + '.platypus.vcf'
          }
      verbosity:
        valueFrom: >
          ${
            return 0;
          }

    out: [output]



```

issue_title: How to change the default storage from S3 to localhost?
issue_body:
Hi,

I have been trying to figure out how to use django-ckeditor with amazon s3 or to adapt my code to support multiple storage backends. The RichTextField works but not with images since for S3 our links expire after a certain amount of time (1 hour). 

Our setup:
 - our default storage: `DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto.S3BotoStorage'`
 - as a security policy we have signed url and they expire after a certain amount of time. (we can not change this for the production bucket)

Since the links expires, the images that I upload within the RichTextField are also available only for a certain amount of time and then they can not be seen anymore. I thought of different ways but the simplest (not nicest) would be to just change the storage backend for the ckeditor field to a FileSystemStorage. 
This can be done on a simple django image/file field: `models.ImageField(storage=FileSystemStorage(location='photos'))`  as explained here http://stackoverflow.com/questions/10419248/django-storages-with-multiple-s3-buckets 

But I did not figure a way to pass it to the RichTextField and did not see it in the settings. Is there a way to do this or a better way to solve my problem?

Thanks,
Vlad

issue_title: Implement event submission form
issue_body:
We need to add a public user-facing form that people can use to submit events to our platform. We should be able to implement this entirely with Django's built-in form utilities. 

To start, you'll want to add three new files to the `dispatch/apps/events` directory: `views.py`, `forms.py` and `urls.py`.

You'll then want to update `dispatch/urls.py` to include the events URLs (similar to what's being done for the API urls).

In the `events/urls.py` file, create a new URL (maybe "submit"?) that connects to a submit view defined in `views.py`

You'll also want to add a new directory `dispatch/apps/events/templates` that contains a single template named "submit.html". You'll use this later to render the form layout.

Read through Django's documentation on forms for a good starting place: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/forms/

issue_title: Replace reference to deprecated ~/.fonts/ folder in installation instructions
issue_body:
The installation instructions for Linux currently specify using the `~/.fonts/` directory, but use of this folder has been deprecated:

https://github.com/behdad/fontconfig/blob/master/fonts.conf.in#L29
https://lists.freedesktop.org/archives/fontconfig/2014-July/005270.html

Instead, it should point to the `~/.local/share/fonts/` folder.

issue_title: Why doesn't Marshmallow Dict field resolve to additionalProperties?
deserialize() throws exception even if strict=False
issue_body:
See https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/2.0.0b4/marshmallow/marshalling.py#L254

Instead of adding the validation error to self.errors, `Unmarshaller` throws an exception directly at this line. This can happen, for example, if the data you're trying to deserialize is a list instead of a dictionary. I think this should be treated like any other validation error and not actually throw the exception here.

issue_title: pre_load needs to repeat validation
issue_body:
`pre_load` is used in all examples as a place to clean up data. But essentially to do a safe clean up, one needs to validate the type and the existence of the key most of the times.
For example in the following example that is from the documentation 
```
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, pre_load

class UserSchema(Schema):
    name = fields.Str()
    slug = fields.Str()

    @pre_load
    def slugify_name(self, in_data):
        in_data['slug'] = in_data['slug'].lower().strip().replace(' ', '-')
        return in_data
```
the following will break without validation error
```
schema = UserSchema().load({'name': 'Steve'})
```
I do not have a clear suggestion but it feels quite problematic and error prone. I our case we are doing in all the cases we use `pre_load` for accessing string fields
```
try:
   ....
except (KeyError, AttributeError):
    pass
```
which is not very elegant. 
Maybe stressing this out in the documentation would be enough.
